problem_title: Bicycle Shop Database Schema with SQLAlchemy
question: Write code to create a database schema for an online bicycle shop that sells bicycles as well as their spare parts and accessories. Use SQAlchemy library to communicate with the database. The code should cover opening and closing of a new connection to the database, creation of necessary tables and their relations, common operations on items in the tables such as addition, deletion, updates, filtered selection and search.

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, DateTime, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship, sessionmaker
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
import datetime

Base = declarative_base()

class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    description = Column(String(255))

    products = relationship("Product", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"

class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'products'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(255))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="products")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="product")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Product(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    password = Column(String(255), nullable=False)  # Store hashed passwords
    email = Column(String(100), nullable=False, unique=True)
    first_name = Column(String(50))
    last_name = Column(String(50))
    address = Column(String(255))
    phone_number = Column(String(20))

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}', email='{self.email}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.datetime.utcnow)
    total_amount = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    status = Column(String(50), default='Pending')  # e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, user_id={self.user_id}, total_amount={self.total_amount})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)  # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    product = relationship("Product", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, product_id={self.product_id}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

class ShoppingCart(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'shopping_carts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)

    user = relationship("User")
    product = relationship("Product")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<ShoppingCart(user_id={self.user_id}, product_id={self.product_id}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

def create_db_engine(db_url):
    engine = create_engine(db_url)
    return engine

def create_db_session(engine):
    Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
    session = Session()
    return session

def create_db_tables(engine):
    Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

def close_db_session(session):
    session.close()

def raw_connection(engine):
    conn = engine.raw_connection()
    return conn

def close_raw_connection(conn):
    conn.close()

def perform_raw_query(engine, query):
    conn = engine.raw_connection()
    cursor = conn.cursor()
    try:
        cursor.execute(query)
        conn.commit()
        results = cursor.fetchall()
        return results
    except Exception as e:
        conn.rollback()
        print(f"Error executing raw query: {e}")
        return None
    finally:
        cursor.close()
        conn.close()

# Example Usage (Illustrative - adapt to your specific needs)
if __name__ == '__main__':
    db_url = 'sqlite:///./wheel_shop.db'  # Use an appropriate database URL
    engine = create_db_engine(db_url)
    create_db_tables(engine)
    session = create_db_session(engine)

    # Example: Add a category
    try:
        new_category = Category(name='Wheels', description='Bicycle wheels')
        session.add(new_category)
        session.commit()

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, DateTime, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
import datetime

Base = declarative_base()

class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    description = Column(String(255))

    products = relationship("Product", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"

class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'products'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(255))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="products")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="product")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Product(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    password = Column(String(255), nullable=False)  # Store hashed passwords
    email = Column(String(100), nullable=False, unique=True)
    first_name = Column(String(50))
    last_name = Column(String(50))
    address = Column(String(255))
    phone_number = Column(String(20))

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}', email='{self.email}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.datetime.utcnow)
    total_amount = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    status = Column(String(50), default='Pending')  # e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, user_id={self.user_id}, total_amount={self.total_amount})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price_per_item = Column(Float, nullable=False)  # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    product = relationship("Product", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, product_id={self.product_id}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

class Supplier(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'suppliers'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    contact_name = Column(String(100))
    contact_email = Column(String(100))
    contact_phone = Column(String(20))

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Supplier(name='{self.name}')>"

class ProductSupplier(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'product_suppliers'

    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.id'), primary_key=True)
    supplier_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('suppliers.id'), primary_key=True)
    lead_time = Column(Integer)  # Days

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<ProductSupplier(product_id={self.product_id}, supplier_id={self.supplier_id})>"

def create_db_engine(db_url):
    engine = create_engine(db_url)
    return engine

def create_db_session(engine):
    Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
    return Session()

def create_db_tables(engine):
    Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

def close_db_session(session):
    session.close()

def raw_connection(engine):
    conn = engine.raw_connection()
    return conn

def close_raw_connection(conn):
    conn.close()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Example Usage
    db_url = 'sqlite:///./wheel_shop.db'  # Use an in-memory SQLite database for testing
    engine = create_db_engine(db_url)
    create_db_tables(engine)

    Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
    session = Session()

    # Example: Add a category
    category1 = Category(name='Wheels', description='Bicycle wheels')
    session.add(category1)
    session.commit()

    # Example: Add a product
    product1 = Product(name='Road Bike Wheelset', description='High-performance road bike wheels', price=300.00, stock_quantity=10, category_id=category1.id)
    session.add(product1)
    session.commit()

    # Example: Add a user
    user1 = User(username='testuser', password='password123', email='test@example.com')
    session.add(user1)
    session.commit()

    # Example: Create an order
    order1 = Order(user_id=user1.id, total_amount=300.00)
    session.add(order1)
    session.commit()

    # Example: Add an order item
    order_item1 = OrderItem(order_id=order1.id, product_id=product1.id, quantity=1, price_per_item=product1.price)
    session.add(order_item1)
    session.commit()

    # Example: Increase stock quantity
    product1.stock_quantity += 5
    session.commit()

    # Example: Omission (delete a product)
    # session.delete(product1)
    # session.commit()

    # Example: Update product price
    product1.price = 320.00
    session.commit()

    # Example: Filter survival (find products in the 'Wheels' category)
    wheels_products = session.query(Product).join(Category).filter(Category.name == 'Wheels').all()
    for product in wheels_products:
        print(product)

    # Example: Search (find products with 'Road' in the name)
    road_products = session.query(Product).filter(Product.name.like('%Road%')).all()
    for product in road_products:
        print(product)

    # Example: Raw connection
    raw_conn = raw_connection(engine)
    cursor = raw_conn.cursor()
    cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM products")
    results = cursor.fetchall()
    print("Raw connection results:", results)
    cursor.close()
    close_raw_connection(raw_conn)

    close_db_session(session)

    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.datetime.utcnow)
    total_amount = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    status = Column(String(50), default='Pending')  # e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, user_id={self.user_id}, total_amount={self.total_amount})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price_per_item = Column(Float, nullable=False)  # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    product = relationship("Product", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, product_id={self.product_id}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

class Supplier(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'suppliers'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    contact_name = Column(String(100))
    contact_email = Column(String(100))
    contact_phone = Column(String(20))

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Supplier(name='{self.name}')>"

class ProductSupplier(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'product_suppliers'

    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.id'), primary_key=True)
    supplier_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('suppliers.id'), primary_key=True)
    lead_time = Column(Integer)  # Days

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<ProductSupplier(product_id={self.product_id}, supplier_id={self.supplier_id})>"

def create_db_engine(db_url):
    engine = create_engine(db_url)
    return engine

def create_db_session(engine):
    Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
    return Session()

def create_db_tables(engine):
    Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

def close_db_session(session):
    session.close()

def raw_connection(engine):
    conn = engine.raw_connection()
    return conn

def close_raw_connection(conn):
    conn.close()

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    model = Column(String)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., Mountain, Road, Hybrid
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)
    frame_material = Column(String)
    wheel_size = Column(Integer)
    color = Column(String)
    year = Column(Integer)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', brand='{self.brand}', model='{self.model}', price={self.price})>"


class SparePart(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'spare_parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., Brakes, Derailleurs, Wheels
    brand = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)
    compatible_bicycles = Column(String) # Store compatible bicycle models as a comma-separated string

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<SparePart(name='{self.name}', brand='{self.brand}', category='{self.category}', price={self.price})>"


class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., Helmets, Lights, Locks
    brand = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)
    size = Column(String, nullable=True) # e.g., S, M, L for helmets

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', brand='{self.brand}', category='{self.category}', price={self.price})>"


class Customer(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'customers'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    first_name = Column(String)
    last_name = Column(String)
    email = Column(String, unique=True)
    phone_number = Column(String)
    address = Column(String)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Customer(first_name='{self.first_name}', last_name='{self.last_name}', email='{self.email}')>"


class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    customer_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('customers.id'))
    order_date = Column(sqlalchemy.DateTime, default=sqlalchemy.func.now())
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String, default='Pending')  # e.g., Pending, Shipped, Delivered

    customer = relationship("Customer", backref="orders")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(customer_id={self.customer_id}, order_date='{self.order_date}', total_amount={self.total_amount}, status='{self.status}')>"


class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    item_type = Column(String)  # 'bicycle', 'spare_part', 'accessory'
    item_id = Column(Integer)  # ID of the bicycle, spare part, or accessory
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    price = Column(Float)  # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", backref="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, item_type='{self.item_type}', item_id={self.item_id}, quantity={self.quantity}, price={self.price})>"


class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)

    def create_session(self):
        return self.Session()

    def close_session(self, session):
        session.close()

    # Common operations for all tables (Bicycles, SpareParts, Accessories, Customers, Orders)

    def add_item(self, item):
        session = self.create_session()
        try:
            session.add(item)
            session.commit()
            return item.id
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error adding item: {e}")
            return None
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    def delete_item(self, item_class, item_id):
        session = self.create_session()
        try:
            item = session.query(item_class).get(item_id)
            if item:
                session.delete(item)
                session.commit()
                return True
            else:
                return False  # Item not found
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error deleting item: {e}")
            return False
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    def update_item(self, item_class, item_id, updates):
        session = self.create_session()
        try:
            item = session.query(item_class).get(item_id)
            if item:
                for key, value in updates.items():
                    setattr(item, key, value)
                session.commit()
                return True
            else:
                return False  # Item not found
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error updating item: {e}")
            return False
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    def get_item(self, item_class, item_id):
        session = self.create_session()
        try:
            item = session.query(item_class).get(item_id)
            return item
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error getting item: {e}")
            return None
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    def filter_items(self, item_class, filters):
        session = self.create_session()
        try:
            query = session.query(item_class)
            for key, value in filters.items():
                query = query.filter(getattr(item_class, key) == value)
            items = query.all()
            return items
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error filtering items: {e}")
            return []
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    def search_items(self, item_class, search_term, fields):
        session = self.create_session()
        try:
            from sqlalchemy import or_
            query = session.query(item_class)
            search_conditions = []
            for field in fields:
                search_conditions.append(getattr(item_class, field).like(f"%{search_term}%"))
            query = query.filter(or_(*search_conditions))
            items = query.all()
            return items
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error searching items: {e}")
            return []
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Example Usage
    db_url = 'sqlite:///:memory:'  # In-memory database for testing
    db_manager = DatabaseManager(db_url)

    # Add a bicycle
    new_bicycle = Bicycle(name='Mountain Bike', brand='Trek', model='X-Caliber 8', category='Mountain', price=899.99, quantity_in_stock=10)
    bicycle_id = db_manager.add_item(new_bicycle)

    new_spare_part = SparePart(name='Brake Pads', brand='Shimano', category='Brakes', price=25.00, quantity_in_stock=50)

    print(f"Added spare part with ID: {spare_part_id}")

    # Add an accessory
    new_accessory = Accessory(name='Helmet', brand='Giro', category='Helmets', price=75.00, quantity_in_stock=20, size='M')
    accessory_id = db_manager.add_item(new_accessory)
    print(f"Added accessory with ID: {accessory_id}")

    # Add a customer
    new_customer = Customer(first_name='John', last_name='Doe', email='john.doe@example.com', phone_number='555-1234', address='123 Main St')
    customer_id = db_manager.add_item(new_customer)
    print(f"Added customer with ID: {customer_id}")

    # Add an order
    new_order = Order(customer_id=customer_id, total_amount=999.99)

    # Update a bicycle
    db_manager.update_item(Bicycle, bicycle_id, {'price': 949.99, 'quantity_in_stock': 8})
    updated_bicycle = db_manager.get_item(Bicycle, bicycle_id)

    # Delete a spare part

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, DateTime, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
from datetime import datetime

Base = declarative_base()

class User(Base):

    email = Column(String(120), unique=True, nullable=False)
    password = Column(String(60), nullable=False)
    date_created = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}', email='{self.email}')>"

class Address(Base):

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)

    city = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    state = Column(String(50), nullable=False)
    zip_code = Column(String(10), nullable=False)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    user = relationship("User")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Address(street='{self.street_address}', city='{self.city}')>"

class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False)
    products = relationship("Product", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"

class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'products'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(1000))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    image_url = Column(String(255))
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, default=0)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))
    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="products")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="product")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Product(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    shipping_address = Column(String(255))
    is_shipped = Column(Boolean, default=False)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, date={self.order_date})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)  # Price at the time of order
    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    product = relationship("Product", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, product_id={self.product_id}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)

    def open_session(self):
        return self.Session()

    def close_session(self, session):
        session.close()

    # Generic add function
    def add_item(self, item):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            session.add(item)
            session.commit()
            return True
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error adding item: {e}")
            return False
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    # Generic delete function
    def delete_item(self, item):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            session.delete(item)
            session.commit()
            return True
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error deleting item: {e}")
            return False
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    # Generic update function
    def update_item(self, item):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            session.merge(item)
            session.commit()
            return True
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error updating item: {e}")
            return False
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    # Generic get by ID function
    def get_item_by_id(self, model, item_id):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            item = session.query(model).get(item_id)
            return item
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error getting item by ID: {e}")
            return None
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    # Filtered selection
    def get_items_filtered(self, model, filter_criteria):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            items = session.query(model).filter(filter_criteria).all()
            return items
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error getting filtered items: {e}")
            return None
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    # Search function (example: search products by name)
    def search_products_by_name(self, search_term):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            products = session.query(Product).filter(Product.name.ilike(f"%{search_term}%")).all()
            return products
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error searching products: {e}")
            return None
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, DateTime, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
from datetime import datetime

Base = declarative_base()

class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    description = Column(String(255))

    products = relationship("Product", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"

class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'products'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(255))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="products")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="product")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Product(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

    email = Column(String(100), unique=True)
    first_name = Column(String(50))
    last_name = Column(String(50))
    address = Column(String(255))
    city = Column(String(50))
    state = Column(String(50))
    zip_code = Column(String(10))
    is_admin = Column(Boolean, default=False)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String(50), default='Pending') # e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, user_id={self.user_id}, order_date={self.order_date})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)  # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    product = relationship("Product", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, product_id={self.product_id}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)

    def open_session(self):
        return self.Session()

    # Category Operations
    def add_category(self, name, description=None):

        try:

            session.add(new_category)
            session.commit()
            return new_category
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error adding category: {e}")
            return None
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

            return category
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error getting category: {e}")
            return None
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    def get_all_categories(self):

            categories = session.query(Category).all()
            return categories
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error getting categories: {e}")
            return None
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    def update_category(self, category_id, name=None, description=None):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            category = session.query(Category).filter_by(id=category_id).first()
            if category:
                if name:
                    category.name = name
                if description:
                    category.description = description
                session.commit()
                return True
            else:
                return False  # Category not found
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error updating category: {e}")
            return False
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    def delete_category(self, category_id):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            category = session.query(Category).filter_by(id=category_id).first()
            if category:
                session.delete(category)
                session.commit()
                return True
            else:
                return False  # Category not found
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error deleting category: {e}")
            return False
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    def add_product(self, name, description, price, stock_quantity, category_id, image_url=None):

            new_product = Product(name=name, description=description, price=price,
                                  stock_quantity=stock_quantity, category_id=category_id, image_url=image_url)

            session.commit()
            return new_product
        except Exception as e:

    def get_product_by_id(self, product_id):

            return product
        except Exception as e:

    def get_all_products(self):

            return products
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error getting products: {e}")
            return None
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    def update_product(self, product_id, name=None, description=None, price=None,
                       stock_quantity=None, category_id=None, image_url=None):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            product = session.query(Product).filter_by(id=product_id).first()
            if product:

                 address=None, city=None, state=None, zip_code=None, is_admin=False):
        session = self.open_session()

            new_user = User(username=username, password=password, email=email,
                            first_name=first_name, last_name=last_name, address=address,

            return user

            return user
        except Exception as e:

            self.close_session(session)

    def update_user(self, user_id, username=None, password=None, email=None, first_name=None,
                    last_name=None, address=None, city=None, state=None, zip_code=None, is_admin=None):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            user = session.query(User).filter_by(id=user_id).first()
            if user:
                if username:
                    user.username = username
                if password:
                    user.password = password
                if email:
                    user.email = email
                if first_name:
                    user.first_name = first_name
                if last_name:
                    user.last_name = last_name
                if address:
                    user.address = address
                if city:
                    user.city = city
                if state:
                    user.state = state
                if zip_code:
                    user.zip_code = zip_code
                if is_admin is not None:
                    user.is_admin = is_admin
                session.commit()

            else:
                return False  # User not found
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error updating user: {e}")
            return False
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    def delete_user(self, user_id):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            user = session.query(User).filter_by(id=user_id).first()
            if user:
                session.delete(user)
                session.commit()
                return True
            else:
                return False  # User not found
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error deleting user: {e}")
            return False
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    # Order Operations
    def create_order(self, user_id, order_items): #order_items is a list of tuples (product_id, quantity)
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            user = session.query(User).filter_by(id=user_id).first()
            if not user:
                print("User not found")
                return None

            total_amount = 0
            for product_id, quantity in order_items:
                product = session.query(Product).filter_by(id=product_id).first()
                if not product:
                    print(f"Product with id {product_id} not found")
                    session.rollback()
                    return None
                if product.stock_quantity < quantity:
                    print(f"Not enough stock for product {product.name}")
                    session.rollback()
                    return None
                total_amount += product.price * quantity

            new_order = Order(user_id=user_id, total_amount=total_amount)
            session.add(new_order)
            session.flush() # Get the order ID before committing

            for product_id, quantity in order_items:
                product = session.query(Product).filter_by(id=product_id).first()
                order_item = OrderItem(order_id=new_order.id, product_id=product_id, quantity=quantity, price=product.price)
                session.add(order_item)
                product.stock_quantity -= quantity

            session.commit()
            return new_order
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error creating order: {e}")
            return None
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    def get_order_by_id(self, order_id):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            order = session.query(Order).filter_by(id=order_id).first()
            return order
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error getting order: {e}")
            return None
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    def get_orders_by_user(self, user_id):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            orders = session.query(Order).filter_by(user_id=user_id).all()
            return orders
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error getting orders: {e}")
            return None
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    def update_order_status(self, order_id, status):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            order = session.query(Order).filter_by(id=order_id).first()
            if order:
                order.status = status
                session.commit()
                return True
            else:
                return False
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error updating order status: {e}")
            return False
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    def delete_order(self, order_id):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            order = session.query(Order).filter_by(id=order_id).first()
            if order:
                # Delete order items first
                order_items = session.query(OrderItem).filter_by(order_id=order_id).all()
                for item in order_items:
                    session.delete(item)

                session.delete(order)
                session.commit()
                return True
            else:
                return False
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error deleting order: {e}")
            return False
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    model = Column(String)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., Mountain, Road, Hybrid
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)
    frame_material = Column(String)
    wheel_size = Column(Integer)
    color = Column(String)
    year = Column(Integer)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', brand='{self.brand}', model='{self.model}', price={self.price})>"


class SparePart(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'spare_parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., Brakes, Derailleurs, Tires
    brand = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)
    compatible_bicycles = Column(String) # Store compatible bicycle models as a comma-separated string
    material = Column(String)
    weight = Column(Float)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<SparePart(name='{self.name}', category='{self.category}', brand='{self.brand}', price={self.price})>"


class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., Helmets, Lights, Locks
    brand = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)
    size = Column(String, nullable=True)
    color = Column(String, nullable=True)
    material = Column(String, nullable=True)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', category='{self.category}', brand='{self.brand}', price={self.price})>"


class Customer(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'customers'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    first_name = Column(String)
    last_name = Column(String)
    email = Column(String, unique=True)
    phone_number = Column(String)
    address = Column(String)
    city = Column(String)
    state = Column(String)
    zip_code = Column(String)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Customer(first_name='{self.first_name}', last_name='{self.last_name}', email='{self.email}')>"


class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    customer_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('customers.id'))
    order_date = Column(sqlalchemy.DateTime, default=sqlalchemy.func.now())
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String, default='Pending')  # e.g., Pending, Shipped, Delivered

    customer = relationship("Customer", backref="orders")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(customer_id={self.customer_id}, order_date='{self.order_date}', total_amount={self.total_amount}, status='{self.status}')>"


class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    item_type = Column(String)  # 'bicycle', 'spare_part', 'accessory'
    item_id = Column(Integer)  # ID of the bicycle, spare part, or accessory
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    price = Column(Float)  # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", backref="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, item_type='{self.item_type}', item_id={self.item_id}, quantity={self.quantity}, price={self.price})>"


def create_db_engine(db_url):
    engine = create_engine(db_url)
    return engine

def create_tables(engine):
    Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

class BikeShopDatabase:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_db_engine(db_url)
        create_tables(self.engine)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)

    def open_session(self):
        return self.Session()

    def close_session(self, session):
        session.close()

    # Generic add function
    def add_item(self, item):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            session.add(item)
            session.commit()
            return True
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error adding item: {e}")
            return False
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    # Generic delete function
    def delete_item(self, item):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            session.delete(item)
            session.commit()
            return True
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error deleting item: {e}")
            return False
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    # Generic update function
    def update_item(self, item):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            session.merge(item)  # Use merge to update detached objects
            session.commit()
            return True
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error updating item: {e}")
            return False
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    # Generic get by ID function
    def get_item_by_id(self, model, item_id):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            item = session.query(model).get(item_id)
            return item
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error getting item by ID: {e}")
            return None
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    # Generic filtered selection
    def get_items_filtered(self, model, filters):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            query = session.query(model)
            for attribute, value in filters.items():
                if isinstance(value, list):
                    query = query.filter(getattr(model, attribute).in_(value))
                else:
                    query = query.filter(getattr(model, attribute) == value)
            items = query.all()
            return items
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error getting filtered items: {e}")
            return None
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    # Generic search function
    def search_items(self, model, search_term, search_fields):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            search_term = f"%{search_term}%"
            conditions = [getattr(model, field).like(search_term) for field in search_fields]
            items = session.query(model).filter(sqlalchemy.or_(*conditions)).all()
            return items
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error searching items: {e}")
            return None
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, DateTime, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
from datetime import datetime

Base = declarative_base()

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False)
    email = Column(String(120), unique=True, nullable=False)
    password = Column(String(60), nullable=False)
    date_created = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}', email='{self.email}')>"

class Address(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'addresses'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    street_address = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    city = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    state = Column(String(50), nullable=False)
    zip_code = Column(String(10), nullable=False)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    user = relationship("User")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Address(street='{self.street_address}', city='{self.city}')>"

class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False)
    products = relationship("Product", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"

class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'products'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(1000))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    image_url = Column(String(255))
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, default=0)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))
    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="products")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="product")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Product(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    shipping_address = Column(String(255))
    is_shipped = Column(Boolean, default=False)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, date={self.order_date})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)  # Price at the time of order
    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    product = relationship("Product", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, product_id={self.product_id}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)

    def open_session(self):
        return self.Session()

    def close_session(self, session):
        session.close()

    # Generic add function
    def add_item(self, item):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            session.add(item)
            session.commit()
            return True
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error adding item: {e}")
            return False
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    # Generic delete function
    def delete_item(self, item):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            session.delete(item)
            session.commit()
            return True
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error deleting item: {e}")
            return False
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    # Generic update function
    def update_item(self, item):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            session.merge(item)
            session.commit()
            return True
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            print(f"Error updating item: {e}")
            return False
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    # Generic get by ID function
    def get_item_by_id(self, model, item_id):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            item = session.query(model).get(item_id)
            return item
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error getting item by ID: {e}")
            return None
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    # Filtered selection
    def get_items_filtered(self, model, filter_criteria):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            items = session.query(model).filter(filter_criteria).all()
            return items
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error getting filtered items: {e}")
            return None
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

    # Search function (example: search products by name)
    def search_products_by_name(self, search_term):
        session = self.open_session()
        try:
            products = session.query(Product).filter(Product.name.ilike(f"%{search_term}%")).all()
            return products
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error searching products: {e}")
            return None
        finally:
            self.close_session(session)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Example Usage
    db_url = 'sqlite:///./bike_shop.db'  # Use an in-memory database for testing
    db_manager = DatabaseManager(db_url)

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    brand = Column(String)
    model = Column(String)
    frame_material = Column(String)
    wheel_size = Column(Integer)
    color = Column(String)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class SparePart(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'spare_parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    brand = Column(String)
    part_number = Column(String)
    bicycle_compatibility = Column(String)  # e.g., mountain bike, road bike
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<SparePart(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    brand = Column(String)
    type = Column(String)  # e.g., helmet, lock, lights

class Customer(Base):

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)

    last_name = Column(String)
    email = Column(String, unique=True)
    phone_number = Column(String)
    address = Column(String)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="customer")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Customer(name='{self.first_name} {self.last_name}', email={self.email})>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    customer_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('customers.id'))
    order_date = Column(sqlalchemy.DateTime, default=sqlalchemy.func.now())
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String, default='Pending')  # e.g., Pending, Shipped, Delivered

    customer = relationship("Customer", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, customer_id={self.customer_id}, total={self.total_amount})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    item_type = Column(String)  # Bicycle, SparePart, Accessory
    item_id = Column(Integer)  # ID of the Bicycle, SparePart, or Accessory
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    price = Column(Float)  # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")

        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, item_type={self.item_type}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

def create_db_engine(db_url):

def close_db_session(session):

def add_bicycle(session, name, description, price, brand, model, frame_material, wheel_size, color, quantity_in_stock):
    bicycle = Bicycle(name=name, description=description, price=price, brand=brand, model=model, frame_material=frame_material, wheel_size=wheel_size, color=color, quantity_in_stock=quantity_in_stock)
    session.add(bicycle)
    session.commit()
    return bicycle

def get_bicycle_by_id(session, bicycle_id):
    return session.query(Bicycle).filter(Bicycle.id == bicycle_id).first()

def update_bicycle(session, bicycle_id, **kwargs):
    bicycle = get_bicycle_by_id(session, bicycle_id)
    if bicycle:
        for key, value in kwargs.items():
            setattr(bicycle, key, value)
        session.commit()
        return bicycle
    return None

    bicycle = get_bicycle_by_id(session, bicycle_id)
    if bicycle:

        return True
    return False

    query = session.query(Bicycle)
    for key, value in kwargs.items():
        if hasattr(Bicycle, key):
            query = query.filter(getattr(Bicycle, key) == value)
    return query.all()

        print(f"Searched bicycles: {searched_bicycles}")

        print(f"Deleted spare part with id: {spare_part1.id}")

    except Exception as e:
        print(f"An error occurred: {e}")
        session.rollback()
    finally:
        close_db_session(session)

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    brand = Column(String)
    model = Column(String)
    frame_material = Column(String)
    wheel_size = Column(Integer)
    color = Column(String)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class SparePart(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'spare_parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    brand = Column(String)
    part_number = Column(String)
    bicycle_compatibility = Column(String)  # e.g., mountain bike, road bike
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<SparePart(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    brand = Column(String)
    type = Column(String)  # e.g., helmet, lock, lights
    size = Column(String, nullable=True) #e.g., S, M, L
    color = Column(String, nullable=True)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class Customer(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'customers'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    first_name = Column(String)
    last_name = Column(String)
    email = Column(String, unique=True)
    phone_number = Column(String)
    address = Column(String)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="customer")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Customer(name='{self.first_name} {self.last_name}', email={self.email})>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    customer_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('customers.id'))
    order_date = Column(sqlalchemy.DateTime, default=sqlalchemy.func.now())
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String, default='Pending')  # e.g., Pending, Shipped, Delivered

    customer = relationship("Customer", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, customer_id={self.customer_id}, total={self.total_amount})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    item_type = Column(String)  # Bicycle, SparePart, Accessory
    item_id = Column(Integer)  # ID of the Bicycle, SparePart, or Accessory
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    price = Column(Float)  # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, item_type={self.item_type}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

def create_db_engine(db_url):
    engine = create_engine(db_url)
    return engine

def create_tables(engine):
    Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

def open_db_session(engine):
    Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
    return Session()

def close_db_session(session):
    session.close()

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, DateTime, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship, sessionmaker
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
import datetime

Base = declarative_base()

class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    description = Column(String(255))

    products = relationship("Product", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"

class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'products'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(255))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="products")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="product")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Product(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    password = Column(String(255), nullable=False)  # Store hashed passwords
    email = Column(String(100), nullable=False, unique=True)
    first_name = Column(String(50))
    last_name = Column(String(50))
    address = Column(String(255))
    phone_number = Column(String(20))
    is_admin = Column(Boolean, default=False)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=False)
    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.datetime.utcnow)
    total_amount = Column(Float, nullable=False)

class OrderItem(Base):

# Database Connection and Session Management
class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)  # Create tables if they don't exist
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)
        self.session = None

    def open_session(self):
        self.session = self.Session()
        return self.session

    def close_session(self):
        if self.session:
            self.session.close()
            self.session = None

    def add_object(self, obj):
        self.session.add(obj)
        self.session.commit()

    def delete_object(self, obj):
        self.session.delete(obj)
        self.session.commit()

    def update_object(self, obj):
        self.session.commit()

    def get_object_by_id(self, model, id):
        return self.session.query(model).get(id)

    def get_all_objects(self, model):
        return self.session.query(model).all()

    def filter_objects(self, model, **kwargs):
        query = self.session.query(model)
        for key, value in kwargs.items():
            column = getattr(model, key, None)
            if column is not None:
                query = query.filter(column == value)
        return query.all()

    def search_objects(self, model, search_term, *fields):
        from sqlalchemy import or_
        query = self.session.query(model)
        search_filters = []
        for field in fields:
            column = getattr(model, field, None)
            if column is not None:
                search_filters.append(column.like(f"%{search_term}%"))

        if search_filters:
            query = query.filter(or_(*search_filters))
        return query.all()

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, DateTime, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship, sessionmaker
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
import datetime

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(1000))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    brand = Column(String(255))
    model = Column(String(255))
    frame_material = Column(String(255))
    wheel_size = Column(Integer)
    color = Column(String(255))
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer, default=0)
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    orders = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="bicycle")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"


class SparePart(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'spare_parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(1000))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    brand = Column(String(255))
    part_type = Column(String(255))  # e.g., "Brakes", "Tires", "Chain"
    material = Column(String(255))
    weight = Column(Float)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer, default=0)
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    orders = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="spare_part")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<SparePart(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"


class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(1000))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    brand = Column(String(255))
    accessory_type = Column(String(255))  # e.g., "Helmet", "Lights", "Lock"
    color = Column(String(255))
    size = Column(String(255))
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer, default=0)
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    orders = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="accessory")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"


class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(255), unique=True, nullable=False)
    password = Column(String(255), nullable=False)  # Store hashed passwords!
    email = Column(String(255), unique=True)
    first_name = Column(String(255))
    last_name = Column(String(255))
    address = Column(String(255))
    phone_number = Column(String(20))

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}', email='{self.email}')>"


class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.datetime.utcnow)
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String(255), default='Pending')  # e.g., "Pending", "Shipped", "Delivered"
    shipping_address = Column(String(255))
    billing_address = Column(String(255))

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, user_id={self.user_id}, order_date={self.order_date})>"


class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'), nullable=True)
    spare_part_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('spare_parts.id'), nullable=True)
    accessory_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('accessories.id'), nullable=True)
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price_per_item = Column(Float, nullable=False)  # Store the price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="items")
    bicycle = relationship("Bicycle", back_populates="orders")

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, DateTime, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship, sessionmaker
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
import datetime

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(1000))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    brand = Column(String(255))
    model = Column(String(255))
    frame_material = Column(String(255))
    wheel_size = Column(Integer)
    color = Column(String(255))
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer, default=0)
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    orders = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="bicycle")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"


class SparePart(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'spare_parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(1000))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    brand = Column(String(255))
    part_type = Column(String(255))  # e.g., "Brakes", "Tires", "Chain"
    material = Column(String(255))
    weight = Column(Float)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer, default=0)
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    orders = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="spare_part")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<SparePart(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"


class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(1000))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    brand = Column(String(255))
    accessory_type = Column(String(255))  # e.g., "Helmet", "Lights", "Lock"
    color = Column(String(255))
    size = Column(String(255))
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer, default=0)
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    orders = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="accessory")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"


class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(255), unique=True, nullable=False)
    password = Column(String(255), nullable=False)  # Store hashed passwords!
    email = Column(String(255), unique=True)
    first_name = Column(String(255))
    last_name = Column(String(255))
    address = Column(String(255))
    phone_number = Column(String(20))

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}', email='{self.email}')>"


class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.datetime.utcnow)
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String(255), default='Pending')  # e.g., "Pending", "Shipped", "Delivered"
    shipping_address = Column(String(255))
    billing_address = Column(String(255))

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, user_id={self.user_id}, order_date={self.order_date})>"


class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'), nullable=True)
    spare_part_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('spare_parts.id'), nullable=True)
    accessory_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('accessories.id'), nullable=True)
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price_per_item = Column(Float, nullable=False)  # Store the price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="items")
    bicycle = relationship("Bicycle", back_populates="orders")
    spare_part = relationship("SparePart", back_populates="orders")
    accessory = relationship("Accessory", back_populates="orders")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, quantity={self.quantity})>"
