import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, DateTime, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
import datetime

Base = declarative_base()

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False)
    email = Column(String(120), unique=True, nullable=False)
    password = Column(String(60), nullable=False)  # Store password hashes, not plain text
    first_name = Column(String(50))
    last_name = Column(String(50))
    address = Column(String(200))
    phone_number = Column(String(20))
    is_admin = Column(Boolean, default=False)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}', email='{self.email}')>"


class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(200))

    products = relationship("Product", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"


class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'products'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(500))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    image_url = Column(String(200))
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, default=0)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))

    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="products")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="product")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Product(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"


class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.datetime.utcnow)
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String(50), default='Pending')  # e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, user_id={self.user_id}, total_amount={self.total_amount})>"


class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)  # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    product = relationship("Product", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, product_id={self.product_id}, quantity={self.quantity})>"


class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)
        self.session = None

    def connect(self):
        if self.session is None:
            self.session = self.Session()
        return self.session

    def close(self):
        if self.session:
            self.session.close()
            self.session = None

    # CRUD operations for Users
    def create_user(self, username, email, password, first_name=None, last_name=None, address=None, phone_number=None, is_admin=False):
        user = User(username=username, email=email, password=password, first_name=first_name, last_name=last_name, address=address, phone_number=phone_number, is_admin=is_admin)
        self.session.add(user)
        self.session.commit()
        return user

    def get_user_by_id(self, user_id):
        return self.session.query(User).filter_by(id=user_id).first()

    def get_user_by_username(self, username):
        return self.session.query(User).filter_by(username=username).first()

    def update_user(self, user_id, **kwargs):
        user = self.get_user_by_id(user_id)
        if user:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(user, key, value)
            self.session.commit()
            return user
        return None

    def delete_user(self, user_id):
        user = self.get_user_by_id(user_id)
        if user:
            self.session.delete(user)
            self.session.commit()
            return True
        return False

    def search_users(self, search_term):
        return self.session.query(User).filter(
            sqlalchemy.or_(
                User.username.like(f"%{search_term}%"),
                User.email.like(f"%{search_term}%"),
                User.first_name.like(f"%{search_term}%"),
                User.last_name.like(f"%{search_term}%")
            )
        ).all()

    # CRUD operations for Categories
    def create_category(self, name, description=None):
        category = Category(name=name, description=description)
        self.session.add(category)
        self.session.commit()
        return category

    def get_category_by_id(self, category_id):
        return self.session.query(Category).filter_by(id=category_id).first()

    def get_category_by_name(self, name):
        return self.session.query(Category).filter_by(name=name).first()

    def update_category(self, category_id, **kwargs):
        category = self.get_category_by_id(category_id)
        if category:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(category, key, value)
            self.session.commit()
            return category
        return None

    def delete_category(self, category_id):
        category = self.get_category_by_id(category_id)
        if category:
            self.session.delete(category)
            self.session.commit()
            return True
        return False

    def search_categories(self, search_term):
        return self.session.query(Category).filter(
            sqlalchemy.or_(
                Category.name.like(f"%{search_term}%"),
                Category.description.like(f"%{search_term}%")
            )
        ).all()

    # CRUD operations for Products
    def create_product(self, name, description, price, image_url, stock_quantity, category_id):
        product = Product(name=name, description=description, price=price, image_url=image_url, stock_quantity=stock_quantity, category_id=category_id)
        self.session.add(product)
        self.session.commit()
        return product

    def get_product_by_id(self, product_id):
        return self.session.query(Product).filter_by(id=product_id).first()

    def update_product(self, product_id, **kwargs):
        product = self.get_product_by_id(product_id)
        if product:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(product, key, value)
            self.session.commit()
            return product
        return None

    def delete_product(self, product_id):
        product = self.get_product_by_id(product_id)
        if product:
            self.session.delete(product)
            self.session.commit()
            return True
        return False

    def search_products(self, search_term):
        return self.session.query(Product).filter(
            sqlalchemy.or_(
                Product.name.like(f"%{search_term}%"),
                Product.description.like(f"%{search_term}%")
            )
        ).all()

    def get_products_by_category(self, category_id):
        return self.session.query(Product).filter_by(category_id=category_id).all()

    # CRUD operations for Orders
    def create_order(self, user_id, total_amount, status='Pending'):
        order = Order(user_id=user_id, order_date=datetime.datetime.utcnow(), total_amount=total_amount, status=status)
        self.session.add(order)
        self.session.commit()
        return order

    def get_order_by_id(self, order_id):
        return self.session.query(Order).filter_by(id=order_id).first()

    def update_order(self, order_id, **kwargs):
        order = self.get_order_by_id(order_id)
        if order:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(order, key, value)
            self.session.commit()
            return order
        return None

    def delete_order(self, order_id):
        order = self.get_order_by_id(order_id)
        if order:
            self.session.delete(order)
            self.session.commit()
            return True
        return False

    def get_orders_by_user(self, user_id):
        return self.session.query(Order).filter_by(user_id=user_id).all()

    # CRUD operations for OrderItems
    def create_order_item(self, order_id, product_id, quantity, price):
        order_item = OrderItem(order_id=order_id, product_id=product_id, quantity=quantity, price=price)
        self.session.add(order_item)
        self.session.commit()
        return order_item

    def get_order_item_by_id(self, order_item_id):
        return self.session.query(OrderItem).filter_by(id=order_item_id).first()

    def update_order_item(self, order_item_id, **kwargs):
        order_item = self.get_order_item_by_id(order_item_id)
        if order_item:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(order_item, key, value)
            self.session.commit()
            return order_item

    def delete_order_item(self, order_item_id):
        order_item = self.get_order_item_by_id(order_item_id)
        if order_item:
            self.session.delete(order_item)
            self.session.commit()
            return True
        return False

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, DateTime, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
import datetime

Base = declarative_base()

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False)
    email = Column(String(120), unique=True, nullable=False)
    password = Column(String(60), nullable=False)  # Store password hashes, not plain text
    first_name = Column(String(50))
    last_name = Column(String(50))
    address = Column(String(200))
    phone_number = Column(String(20))
    is_admin = Column(Boolean, default=False)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}', email='{self.email}')>"


class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(200))

    products = relationship("Product", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"


class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'products'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(500))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    image_url = Column(String(200))
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, default=0)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))

    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="products")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="product")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Product(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"


class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.datetime.utcnow)
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String(50), default='Pending')  # e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, user_id={self.user_id}, total_amount={self.total_amount})>"


class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)  # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    product = relationship("Product", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, product_id={self.product_id}, quantity={self.quantity})>"


class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)
        self.session = None

    def connect(self):
        if self.session is None:
            self.session = self.Session()
        return self.session

    def close(self):
        if self.session:
            self.session.close()
            self.session = None

    # CRUD operations for Users
    def create_user(self, username, email, password, first_name=None, last_name=None, address=None, phone_number=None, is_admin=False):
        user = User(username=username, email=email, password=password, first_name=first_name, last_name=last_name, address=address, phone_number=phone_number, is_admin=is_admin)
        self.session.add(user)
        self.session.commit()
        return user

        return self.session.query(User).filter_by(id=user_id).first()

        return self.session.query(User).filter_by(username=username).first()

    def update_user(self, user_id, **kwargs):
        user = self.get_user_by_id(user_id)
        if user:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(user, key, value)
            self.session.commit()
            return user
        return None

    def delete_user(self, user_id):
        user = self.get_user_by_id(user_id)
        if user:
            self.session.delete(user)
            self.session.commit()
            return True
        return False

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    description = Column(String(255))

    items = relationship("Item", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"

class Item(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(255))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="items")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="item")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Item(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    password = Column(String(100), nullable=False)  # Store hashed passwords
    email = Column(String(100), unique=True)
    first_name = Column(String(50))
    last_name = Column(String(50))
    address = Column(String(255))
    city = Column(String(50))
    state = Column(String(50))
    zip_code = Column(String(10))

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    order_date = Column(sqlalchemy.DateTime, default=sqlalchemy.func.now())
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String(50), default='Pending')  # e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, user_id={self.user_id})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    item_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('items.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)  # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    item = relationship("Item", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, item_id={self.item_id})>"

# Database Connection
DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///./bike_shop.db"  # Use SQLite for simplicity; can be changed to PostgreSQL, MySQL, etc.

engine = create_engine(DATABASE_URL)
Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)

# Example Usage (CRUD operations)
def get_db():
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        yield db
    finally:
        db.close()

def create_category(db: SessionLocal, name: str, description: str = None):
    db_category = Category(name=name, description=description)
    db.add(db_category)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_category)
    return db_category

def get_category(db: SessionLocal, category_id: int):
    return db.query(Category).filter(Category.id == category_id).first()

def get_category_by_name(db: SessionLocal, category_name: str):
    return db.query(Category).filter(Category.name == category_name).first()

def get_categories(db: SessionLocal, skip: int = 0, limit: int = 100):
    return db.query(Category).offset(skip).limit(limit).all()

def update_category(db: SessionLocal, category_id: int, name: str = None, description: str = None):
    db_category = get_category(db, category_id)
    if db_category:
        if name:
            db_category.name = name
        if description:
            db_category.description = description
        db.commit()
        db.refresh(db_category)
    return db_category

def delete_category(db: SessionLocal, category_id: int):
    db_category = get_category(db, category_id)
    if db_category:
        db.delete(db_category)
        db.commit()
        return True
    return False

def create_item(db: SessionLocal, name: str, description: str, price: float, stock_quantity: int, category_id: int, image_url: str = None):
    db_item = Item(name=name, description=description, price=price, stock_quantity=stock_quantity, category_id=category_id, image_url=image_url)
    db.add(db_item)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_item)
    return db_item

def get_item(db: SessionLocal, item_id: int):
    return db.query(Item).filter(Item.id == item_id).first()

def get_items(db: SessionLocal, skip: int = 0, limit: int = 100):
    return db.query(Item).offset(skip).limit(limit).all()

def update_item(db: SessionLocal, item_id: int, name: str = None, description: str = None, price: float = None, stock_quantity: int = None, category_id: int = None, image_url: str = None):
    db_item = get_item(db, item_id)
    if db_item:
        if name:
            db_item.name = name
        if description:
            db_item.description = description
        if price is not None:  # Allow setting price to 0
            db_item.price = price
        if stock_quantity is not None: # Allow setting stock to 0
            db_item.stock_quantity = stock_quantity
        if category_id:
            db_item.category_id = category_id
        if image_url:
            db_item.image_url = image_url
        db.commit()
        db.refresh(db_item)
    return db_item

def delete_item(db: SessionLocal, item_id: int):
    db_item = get_item(db, item_id)
    if db_item:
        db.delete(db_item)
        db.commit()
        return True
    return False

def search_items(db: SessionLocal, query: str):
    return db.query(Item).filter(Item.name.contains(query) | Item.description.contains(query)).all()

def create_user(db: SessionLocal, username: str, password: str, email: str, first_name: str = None, last_name: str = None, address: str = None, city: str = None, state: str = None, zip_code: str = None):
    db_user = User(username=username, password=password, email=email, first_name=first_name, last_name=last_name, address=address, city=city, state=state, zip_code=zip_code)
    db.add(db_user)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_user)
    return db_user

def get_user(db: SessionLocal, user_id: int):
    return db.query(User).filter(User.id == user_id).first()

def get_user_by_username(db: SessionLocal, username: str):
    return db.query(User).filter(User.username == username).first()

def create_order(db: SessionLocal, user_id: int):
    db_order = Order(user_id=user_id)
    db.add(db_order)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_order)
    return db_order

def get_order(db: SessionLocal, order_id: int):
    return db.query(Order).filter(Order.id == order_id).first()

def add_item_to_order(db: SessionLocal, order_id: int, item_id: int, quantity: int):
    order = get_order(db, order_id)
    item = get_item(db, item_id)

    if not order or not item:
        return None

    order_item = OrderItem(order_id=order_id, item_id=item_id, quantity=quantity, price=item.price)
    db.add(order_item)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(order_item)

    # Update total amount of the order
    order.total_amount = sum(oi.quantity * oi.price for oi in order.order_items)
    db.commit()

    return order_item

def get_order_items(db: SessionLocal, order_id: int):
    return db.query(OrderItem).filter(OrderItem.order_id == order_id).all()

def update_order_status(db: SessionLocal, order_id: int, status: str):
    order = get_order(db, order_id)
    if order:
        order.status = status
        db.commit()
        db.refresh(order)
    return order

# Example usage:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    db = next(get_db())

    # Create categories
    category1 = create_category(db, name="Bikes", description="Complete bicycles")
    category2 = create_category(db, name="Components", description="Individual bike parts")

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    description = Column(String(255))

    items = relationship("Item", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"

class Item(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(255))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="items")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="item")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Item(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    password = Column(String(100), nullable=False)  # Store hashed passwords
    email = Column(String(100), unique=True)
    first_name = Column(String(50))
    last_name = Column(String(50))
    address = Column(String(255))
    city = Column(String(50))
    state = Column(String(50))
    zip_code = Column(String(10))

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    order_date = Column(sqlalchemy.DateTime, default=sqlalchemy.func.now())
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String(50), default='Pending')  # e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, user_id={self.user_id})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    item_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('items.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)  # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    item = relationship("Item", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, item_id={self.item_id})>"

# Database Connection
DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///./bike_shop.db"  # Use SQLite for simplicity; can be changed to PostgreSQL, MySQL, etc.

engine = create_engine(DATABASE_URL)
Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)

# Example Usage (CRUD operations)
def get_db():
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        yield db
    finally:
        db.close()

def create_category(db: SessionLocal, name: str, description: str = None):
    db_category = Category(name=name, description=description)
    db.add(db_category)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_category)
    return db_category

def get_category(db: SessionLocal, category_id: int):
    return db.query(Category).filter(Category.id == category_id).first()

def get_category_by_name(db: SessionLocal, category_name: str):
    return db.query(Category).filter(Category.name == category_name).first()

def get_categories(db: SessionLocal, skip: int = 0, limit: int = 100):
    return db.query(Category).offset(skip).limit(limit).all()

def update_category(db: SessionLocal, category_id: int, name: str = None, description: str = None):
    db_category = get_category(db, category_id)
    if db_category:
        if name:
            db_category.name = name
        if description:
            db_category.description = description
        db.commit()
        db.refresh(db_category)
    return db_category

def delete_category(db: SessionLocal, category_id: int):
    db_category = get_category(db, category_id)
    if db_category:
        db.delete(db_category)
        db.commit()
        return True
    return False

def create_item(db: SessionLocal, name: str, description: str, price: float, stock_quantity: int, category_id: int, image_url: str = None):
    db_item = Item(name=name, description=description, price=price, stock_quantity=stock_quantity, category_id=category_id, image_url=image_url)
    db.add(db_item)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_item)
    return db_item

def get_item(db: SessionLocal, item_id: int):
    return db.query(Item).filter(Item.id == item_id).first()

def get_items(db: SessionLocal, skip: int = 0, limit: int = 100):
    return db.query(Item).offset(skip).limit(limit).all()

def update_item(db: SessionLocal, item_id: int, name: str = None, description: str = None, price: float = None, stock_quantity: int = None, category_id: int = None, image_url: str = None):
    db_item = get_item(db, item_id)
    if db_item:
        if name:
            db_item.name = name
        if description:
            db_item.description = description
        if price is not None:  # Allow setting price to 0
            db_item.price = price
        if stock_quantity is not None: # Allow setting stock to 0
            db_item.stock_quantity = stock_quantity
        if category_id:
            db_item.category_id = category_id
        if image_url:
            db_item.image_url = image_url
        db.commit()
        db.refresh(db_item)
    return db_item

def delete_item(db: SessionLocal, item_id: int):
    db_item = get_item(db, item_id)
    if db_item:
        db.delete(db_item)
        db.commit()
        return True
    return False

def search_items(db: SessionLocal, query: str):
    return db.query(Item).filter(Item.name.contains(query) | Item.description.contains(query)).all()

def create_user(db: SessionLocal, username: str, password: str, email: str, first_name: str = None, last_name: str = None, address: str = None, city: str = None, state: str = None, zip_code: str = None):
    db_user = User(username=username, password=password, email=email, first_name=first_name, last_name=last_name, address=address, city=city, state=state, zip_code=zip_code)
    db.add(db_user)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_user)
    return db_user

def get_user(db: SessionLocal, user_id: int):
    return db.query(User).filter(User.id == user_id).first()

def get_user_by_username(db: SessionLocal, username: str):
    return db.query(User).filter(User.username == username).first()

def create_order(db: SessionLocal, user_id: int):
    db_order = Order(user_id=user_id)
    db.add(db_order)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_order)
    return db_order

def get_order(db: SessionLocal, order_id: int):
    return db.query(Order).filter(Order.id == order_id).first()

def add_item_to_order(db: SessionLocal, order_id: int, item_id: int, quantity: int):
    order = get_order(db, order_id)
    item = get_item(db, item_id)

    if not order or not item:
        return None

    order_item = OrderItem(order_id=order_id, item_id=item_id, quantity=quantity, price=item.price)
    db.add(order_item)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(order_item)

    # Update total amount of the order
    order.total_amount = sum(oi.quantity * oi.price for oi in order.order_items)
    db.commit()

    return order_item

def get_order_items(db: SessionLocal, order_id: int):
    return db.query(OrderItem).filter(OrderItem.order_id == order_id).all()

def update_order_status(db: SessionLocal, order_id: int, status: str):
    order = get_order(db, order_id)
    if order:
        order.status = status
        db.commit()
        db.refresh(order)
    return order

# Example usage:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    db = next(get_db())

    # Create categories
    category1 = create_category(db, name="Bikes", description="Complete bicycles")
    category2 = create_category(db, name="Components", description="Individual bike parts")

    item4 = create_item(db, name="Bike Helmet", description="Protective bike helmet", price=50.00, stock_quantity=30, category_id=category3.id, image_url="helmet.jpg")

    # Create a user
    user1 = create_user(db, username="johndoe", password="password123", email="john.doe@example.com", first_name="John", last_name="Doe")

    # Create an order
    order1 = create_order(db, user_id=user1.id)

    # Add items to the order
    add_item_to_order(db, order1.id, item1.id, 1)
    add_item_to_order(db, order1.id, item4.id, 2)

    # Get order items
    order_items = get_order_items(db, order1.id)
    print(f"Order items for order {order1.id}: {order_items}")

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    description = Column(String(255))

    items = relationship("Item", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"

class Item(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(255))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="items")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="item")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Item(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    password = Column(String(100), nullable=False)  # Store hashed passwords
    email = Column(String(100), unique=True)
    first_name = Column(String(50))
    last_name = Column(String(50))
    is_admin = Column(Boolean, default=False)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=False)
    order_date = Column(sqlalchemy.DateTime, default=sqlalchemy.func.now())
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String(50), default='Pending') # e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, user_id={self.user_id})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'), nullable=False)
    item_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('items.id'), nullable=False)
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price_per_item = Column(Float, nullable=False) # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    item = relationship("Item", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, item_id={self.item_id}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    description = Column(String(255))

    items = relationship("Item", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"

class Item(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(255))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="items")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="item")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Item(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    password = Column(String(100), nullable=False)  # Store hashed passwords
    email = Column(String(100), unique=True)
    first_name = Column(String(50))
    last_name = Column(String(50))
    address = Column(String(255))
    city = Column(String(50))
    state = Column(String(50))
    zip_code = Column(String(10))

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    order_date = Column(sqlalchemy.DateTime, default=sqlalchemy.func.now())
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String(50), default='Pending')  # e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, user_id={self.user_id})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    item_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('items.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)  # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    item = relationship("Item", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, item_id={self.item_id})>"

# Database Connection
DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///./bike_shop.db"  # Use SQLite for simplicity; can be changed to PostgreSQL, MySQL, etc.

engine = create_engine(DATABASE_URL)
Base.metadata.create_all(engine)

SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)

# Example Usage (CRUD operations)
def get_db():
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        yield db
    finally:
        db.close()

def create_category(db: SessionLocal, name: str, description: str = None):
    db_category = Category(name=name, description=description)
    db.add(db_category)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_category)
    return db_category

def get_category(db: SessionLocal, category_id: int):
    return db.query(Category).filter(Category.id == category_id).first()

def get_category_by_name(db: SessionLocal, category_name: str):
    return db.query(Category).filter(Category.name == category_name).first()

def get_categories(db: SessionLocal, skip: int = 0, limit: int = 100):
    return db.query(Category).offset(skip).limit(limit).all()

def update_category(db: SessionLocal, category_id: int, name: str = None, description: str = None):
    db_category = get_category(db, category_id)
    if db_category:
        if name:
            db_category.name = name
        if description:
            db_category.description = description
        db.commit()
        db.refresh(db_category)
    return db_category

def delete_category(db: SessionLocal, category_id: int):
    db_category = get_category(db, category_id)
    if db_category:
        db.delete(db_category)
        db.commit()
        return True
    return False

def create_item(db: SessionLocal, name: str, description: str, price: float, stock_quantity: int, category_id: int, image_url: str = None):
    db_item = Item(name=name, description=description, price=price, stock_quantity=stock_quantity, category_id=category_id, image_url=image_url)
    db.add(db_item)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_item)
    return db_item

def get_item(db: SessionLocal, item_id: int):
    return db.query(Item).filter(Item.id == item_id).first()

def get_items(db: SessionLocal, skip: int = 0, limit: int = 100):
    return db.query(Item).offset(skip).limit(limit).all()

def update_item(db: SessionLocal, item_id: int, name: str = None, description: str = None, price: float = None, stock_quantity: int = None, category_id: int = None, image_url: str = None):
    db_item = get_item(db, item_id)
    if db_item:
        if name:
            db_item.name = name
        if description:
            db_item.description = description
        if price is not None:  # Allow setting price to 0
            db_item.price = price
        if stock_quantity is not None: # Allow setting stock to 0
            db_item.stock_quantity = stock_quantity
        if category_id:
            db_item.category_id = category_id
        if image_url:
            db_item.image_url = image_url
        db.commit()
        db.refresh(db_item)
    return db_item

def delete_item(db: SessionLocal, item_id: int):
    db_item = get_item(db, item_id)
    if db_item:
        db.delete(db_item)
        db.commit()
        return True
    return False

def search_items(db: SessionLocal, query: str):
    return db.query(Item).filter(Item.name.contains(query) | Item.description.contains(query)).all()

def create_user(db: SessionLocal, username: str, password: str, email: str, first_name: str = None, last_name: str = None, address: str = None, city: str = None, state: str = None, zip_code: str = None):
    db_user = User(username=username, password=password, email=email, first_name=first_name, last_name=last_name, address=address, city=city, state=state, zip_code=zip_code)
    db.add(db_user)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_user)
    return db_user

def get_user(db: SessionLocal, user_id: int):
    return db.query(User).filter(User.id == user_id).first()

def get_user_by_username(db: SessionLocal, username: str):
    return db.query(User).filter(User.username == username).first()

def create_order(db: SessionLocal, user_id: int):
    db_order = Order(user_id=user_id)
    db.add(db_order)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(db_order)
    return db_order

def get_order(db: SessionLocal, order_id: int):
    return db.query(Order).filter(Order.id == order_id).first()

def add_item_to_order(db: SessionLocal, order_id: int, item_id: int, quantity: int):
    order = get_order(db, order_id)
    item = get_item(db, item_id)

    if not order or not item:
        return None

    order_item = OrderItem(order_id=order_id, item_id=item_id, quantity=quantity, price=item.price)
    db.add(order_item)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(order_item)

    # Update total amount of the order
    order.total_amount = sum(oi.quantity * oi.price for oi in order.order_items)
    db.commit()

    return order_item

def get_order_items(db: SessionLocal, order_id: int):
    return db.query(OrderItem).filter(OrderItem.order_id == order_id).all()

def update_order_status(db: SessionLocal, order_id: int, status: str):
    order = get_order(db, order_id)
    if order:
        order.status = status
        db.commit()
        db.refresh(order)
    return order

# Example usage:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    db = next(get_db())

    # Create categories
    category1 = create_category(db, name="Bikes", description="Complete bicycles")
    category2 = create_category(db, name="Components", description="Individual bike parts")
    category3 = create_category(db, name="Accessories", description="Bike-related accessories")

    # Create items
    item1 = create_item(db, name="Mountain Bike", description="Full suspension mountain bike", price=1500.00, stock_quantity=10, category_id=category1.id, image_url="mountain_bike.jpg")
    item2 = create_item(db, name="Road Bike", description="Lightweight road racing bike", price=1200.00, stock_quantity=5, category_id=category1.id, image_url="road_bike.jpg")
    item3 = create_item(db, name="Shimano Derailleur", description="High-performance rear derailleur", price=80.00, stock_quantity=20, category_id=category2.id, image_url="derailleur.jpg")
    item4 = create_item(db, name="Bike Helmet", description="Protective bike helmet", price=50.00, stock_quantity=30, category_id=category3.id, image_url="helmet.jpg")

    # Create a user
    user1 = create_user(db, username="johndoe", password="password123", email="john.doe@example.com", first_name="John", last_name="Doe")

    # Create an order
    order1 = create_order(db, user_id=user1.id)

    # Add items to the order
    add_item_to_order(db, order1.id, item1.id, 1)
    add_item_to_order(db, order1.id, item4.id, 2)

    # Get order items
    order_items = get_order_items(db, order1.id)
    print(f"Order items for order {order1.id}: {order_items}")

    # Update order status

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    description = Column(String(255))

    items = relationship("Item", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"

class Item(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(255))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="items")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="item")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Item(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), nullable=False, unique=True)
    password = Column(String(100), nullable=False)  # Store hashed passwords
    email = Column(String(100), unique=True)
    first_name = Column(String(50))
    last_name = Column(String(50))
    is_admin = Column(Boolean, default=False)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=False)
    order_date = Column(sqlalchemy.DateTime, default=sqlalchemy.func.now())
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String(50), default='Pending') # e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, user_id={self.user_id})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'), nullable=False)
    item_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('items.id'), nullable=False)
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price_per_item = Column(Float, nullable=False) # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    item = relationship("Item", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, item_id={self.item_id}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    model = Column(String)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., Road, Mountain, Hybrid
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)
    frame_material = Column(String)
    wheel_size = Column(Integer)
    color = Column(String)
    gender = Column(String) # Male, Female, Unisex

    images = relationship("BicycleImage", back_populates="bicycle")
    reviews = relationship("Review", back_populates="bicycle")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="bicycle")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', brand='{self.brand}', model='{self.model}', price={self.price})>"

class BicycleImage(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycle_images'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))
    image_url = Column(String)

    bicycle = relationship("Bicycle", back_populates="images")

class Part(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., Brakes, Drivetrain, Wheels
    brand = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)
    weight = Column(Float)
    compatible_bicycles = Column(String) #Store compatible bicycle models as a comma-separated string

    images = relationship("PartImage", back_populates="part")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="part")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Part(name='{self.name}', brand='{self.brand}', category='{self.category}', price={self.price})>"

class PartImage(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'part_images'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    part_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('parts.id'))
    image_url = Column(String)

    part = relationship("Part", back_populates="images")

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., Helmets, Lights, Locks
    brand = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)

class Review(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'reviews'

class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)

    def open_session(self):
        return self.Session()

    def close_session(self, session):

    # CRUD operations for Bicycles
    def add_bicycle(self, session, name, brand, model, category, description, price, quantity_in_stock, frame_material, wheel_size, color, gender):
        bicycle = Bicycle(name=name, brand=brand, model=model, category=category, description=description, price=price, quantity_in_stock=quantity_in_stock, frame_material=frame_material, wheel_size=wheel_size, color=color, gender=gender)
        session.add(bicycle)
        session.commit()
        return bicycle

    def get_bicycle_by_id(self, session, bicycle_id):
        return session.query(Bicycle).filter(Bicycle.id == bicycle_id).first()

    def update_bicycle(self, session, bicycle_id, **kwargs):
        bicycle = self.get_bicycle_by_id(session, bicycle_id)
        if bicycle:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(bicycle, key, value)
            session.commit()
            return True
        return False

    def delete_bicycle(self, session, bicycle_id):
        bicycle = self.get_bicycle_by_id(session, bicycle_id)
        if bicycle:
            session.delete(bicycle)
            session.commit()
            return True
        return False

        search_term = f"%{search_term}%"
        return session.query(Bicycle).filter(
            (Bicycle.name.like(search_term)) |
            (Bicycle.brand.like(search_term)) |
            (Bicycle.model.like(search_term)) |
            (Bicycle.description.like(search_term))
        ).all()

        query = session.query(Bicycle)
        for key, value in filters.items():
            if hasattr(Bicycle, key):
                query = query.filter(getattr(Bicycle, key) == value)

            return None #Email already exists

            try:
                session.commit()
                return True
            except sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError:
                session.rollback()
                return False #Email already exists

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    model = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., "Mountain", "Road", "Hybrid"
    frame_material = Column(String)
    wheel_size = Column(Integer)
    color = Column(String)
    gender = Column(String) # "Male", "Female", "Unisex"
    year = Column(Integer)

    images = relationship("BicycleImage", back_populates="bicycle")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="bicycle")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', brand='{self.brand}', model='{self.model}', price={self.price})>"

class BicycleImage(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycle_images'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))
    image_url = Column(String)

    bicycle = relationship("Bicycle", back_populates="images")

class Part(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., "Tires", "Brakes", "Gears"
    brand = Column(String)
    weight = Column(Float)
    compatible_bicycles = Column(String) #Store compatible bicycle models as comma separated string

    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="part")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Part(name='{self.name}', category='{self.category}', price={self.price})>"

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., "Helmets", "Lights", "Locks"
    brand = Column(String)
    color = Column(String)
    size = Column(String)

    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="accessory")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', category='{self.category}', price={self.price})>"

class Customer(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'customers'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    first_name = Column(String)
    last_name = Column(String)
    email = Column(String)
    phone_number = Column(String)
    address = Column(String)
    city = Column(String)
    state = Column(String)
    zip_code = Column(String)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="customer")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Customer(first_name='{self.first_name}', last_name='{self.last_name}', email='{self.email}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    customer_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('customers.id'))
    order_date = Column(String) #Store as string for simplicity
    shipping_address = Column(String)
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String) # e.g., "Pending", "Shipped", "Delivered"

    customer = relationship("Customer", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(customer_id={self.customer_id}, order_date='{self.order_date}', total_amount={self.total_amount})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'), nullable=True)
    part_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('parts.id'), nullable=True)
    accessory_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('accessories.id'), nullable=True)
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    price = Column(Float) #Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    bicycle = relationship("Bicycle", back_populates="order_items")
    part = relationship("Part", back_populates="order_items")
    accessory = relationship("Accessory", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, quantity={self.quantity}, price={self.price})>"

class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)

    def open_session(self):
        return self.Session()

    def close_session(self, session):
        session.close()

    # CRUD operations for Bicycles
    def add_bicycle(self, session, name, brand, model, description, price, quantity, category, frame_material, wheel_size, color, gender, year):
        bicycle = Bicycle(name=name, brand=brand, model=model, description=description, price=price, quantity=quantity, category=category, frame_material=frame_material, wheel_size=wheel_size, color=color, gender=gender, year=year)
        session.add(bicycle)
        session.commit()
        return bicycle

    def get_bicycle(self, session, bicycle_id):
        return session.query(Bicycle).get(bicycle_id)

    def update_bicycle(self, session, bicycle_id, **kwargs):
        bicycle = self.get_bicycle(session, bicycle_id)
        if bicycle:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(bicycle, key, value)
            session.commit()
            return bicycle
        return None

    def delete_bicycle(self, session, bicycle_id):
        bicycle = self.get_bicycle(session, bicycle_id)
        if bicycle:
            session.delete(bicycle)
            session.commit()
            return True
        return False

    def search_bicycles(self, session, search_term):
        search_term = f"%{search_term}%"
        return session.query(Bicycle).filter(
            sqlalchemy.or_(
                Bicycle.name.like(search_term),
                Bicycle.brand.like(search_term),
                Bicycle.model.like(search_term),
                Bicycle.description.like(search_term)
            )
        ).all()

    def filter_bicycles(self, session, **filters):
        query = session.query(Bicycle)
        for key, value in filters.items():
            if hasattr(Bicycle, key):
                query = query.filter(getattr(Bicycle, key) == value)
        return query.all()

    def search_parts(self, session, search_term):

                Part.name.like(search_term),
                Part.description.like(search_term),
                Part.brand.like(search_term),
                Part.category.like(search_term)
            )
        ).all()

    def filter_parts(self, session, **filters):
        query = session.query(Part)
        for key, value in filters.items():
            if hasattr(Part, key):
                query = query.filter(getattr(Part, key) == value)

    # CRUD operations for Accessories
    def add_accessory(self, session, name, description, price, quantity, category, brand, color, size):

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    model = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., "Mountain", "Road", "Hybrid"
    frame_material = Column(String)
    wheel_size = Column(Integer)
    color = Column(String)
    gender = Column(String) # "Male", "Female", "Unisex"
    year = Column(Integer)

    images = relationship("BicycleImage", back_populates="bicycle")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="bicycle")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', brand='{self.brand}', model='{self.model}', price={self.price})>"

class BicycleImage(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycle_images'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))
    image_url = Column(String)

    bicycle = relationship("Bicycle", back_populates="images")

class Part(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., "Tires", "Brakes", "Gears"
    brand = Column(String)
    weight = Column(Float)
    compatible_bicycles = Column(String) #Store compatible bicycle models as comma separated string

    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="part")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Part(name='{self.name}', category='{self.category}', price={self.price})>"

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., "Helmets", "Lights", "Locks"
    brand = Column(String)
    color = Column(String)
    size = Column(String)

    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="accessory")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', category='{self.category}', price={self.price})>"

class Customer(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'customers'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    first_name = Column(String)
    last_name = Column(String)
    email = Column(String)
    phone_number = Column(String)
    address = Column(String)
    city = Column(String)
    state = Column(String)
    zip_code = Column(String)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="customer")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Customer(first_name='{self.first_name}', last_name='{self.last_name}', email='{self.email}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    customer_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('customers.id'))
    order_date = Column(String) #Store as string for simplicity
    shipping_address = Column(String)
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String) # e.g., "Pending", "Shipped", "Delivered"

    customer = relationship("Customer", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(customer_id={self.customer_id}, order_date='{self.order_date}', total_amount={self.total_amount})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'), nullable=True)
    part_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('parts.id'), nullable=True)
    accessory_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('accessories.id'), nullable=True)
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    price = Column(Float) #Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    bicycle = relationship("Bicycle", back_populates="order_items")
    part = relationship("Part", back_populates="order_items")
    accessory = relationship("Accessory", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, quantity={self.quantity}, price={self.price})>"

class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)

    def open_session(self):
        return self.Session()

    def close_session(self, session):
        session.close()

    # CRUD operations for Bicycles
    def add_bicycle(self, session, name, brand, model, description, price, quantity, category, frame_material, wheel_size, color, gender, year):
        bicycle = Bicycle(name=name, brand=brand, model=model, description=description, price=price, quantity=quantity, category=category, frame_material=frame_material, wheel_size=wheel_size, color=color, gender=gender, year=year)
        session.add(bicycle)
        session.commit()
        return bicycle

    def get_bicycle(self, session, bicycle_id):
        return session.query(Bicycle).get(bicycle_id)

    def update_bicycle(self, session, bicycle_id, **kwargs):
        bicycle = self.get_bicycle(session, bicycle_id)
        if bicycle:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(bicycle, key, value)
            session.commit()
            return bicycle
        return None

    def delete_bicycle(self, session, bicycle_id):
        bicycle = self.get_bicycle(session, bicycle_id)
        if bicycle:
            session.delete(bicycle)
            session.commit()
            return True
        return False

        search_term = f"%{search_term}%"
        return session.query(Bicycle).filter(

            )
        ).all()

        query = session.query(Bicycle)
        for key, value in filters.items():

                query = query.filter(getattr(Bicycle, key) == value)
        return query.all()

        query = session.query(Part)
        for key, value in filters.items():

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    model = Column(String)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., Road, Mountain, Hybrid
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)
    frame_material = Column(String)
    wheel_size = Column(Integer)
    color = Column(String)
    gender = Column(String) # Male, Female, Unisex

    images = relationship("BicycleImage", back_populates="bicycle")
    reviews = relationship("Review", back_populates="bicycle")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="bicycle")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', brand='{self.brand}', model='{self.model}', price={self.price})>"

class BicycleImage(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycle_images'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))
    image_url = Column(String)

    bicycle = relationship("Bicycle", back_populates="images")

class Part(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., Brakes, Drivetrain, Wheels

class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)

    def open_session(self):
        return self.Session()

    def close_session(self, session):
        session.close()

    # CRUD operations for Bicycles
    def add_bicycle(self, session, name, brand, model, category, description, price, quantity_in_stock, frame_material, wheel_size, color, gender):
        bicycle = Bicycle(name=name, brand=brand, model=model, category=category, description=description, price=price, quantity_in_stock=quantity_in_stock, frame_material=frame_material, wheel_size=wheel_size, color=color, gender=gender)
        session.add(bicycle)
        session.commit()
        return bicycle

    def get_bicycle_by_id(self, session, bicycle_id):
        return session.query(Bicycle).filter(Bicycle.id == bicycle_id).first()

    def update_bicycle(self, session, bicycle_id, **kwargs):
        bicycle = self.get_bicycle_by_id(session, bicycle_id)
        if bicycle:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(bicycle, key, value)
            session.commit()
            return True
        return False

    def delete_bicycle(self, session, bicycle_id):
        bicycle = self.get_bicycle_by_id(session, bicycle_id)
        if bicycle:
            session.delete(bicycle)
            session.commit()
            return True
        return False

    def search_bicycles(self, session, search_term):
        search_term = f"%{search_term}%"
        return session.query(Bicycle).filter(
            (Bicycle.name.like(search_term)) |
            (Bicycle.brand.like(search_term)) |
            (Bicycle.model.like(search_term)) |
            (Bicycle.description.like(search_term))
        ).all()

    def filter_bicycles(self, session, **filters):
        query = session.query(Bicycle)
        for key, value in filters.items():
            if hasattr(Bicycle, key):
                query = query.filter(getattr(Bicycle, key) == value)
        return query.all()

        session.add(customer)
        try:
            session.commit()
            return customer
        except sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError:
            session.rollback()
            return None #Email already exists

    def get_customer_by_id(self, session, customer_id):
        return session.query(Customer).filter(Customer.id == customer_id).first()

    def update_customer(self, session, customer_id, **kwargs):
        customer = self.get_customer_by_id(session, customer_id)
        if customer:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(customer, key, value)
            try:
                session.commit()
                return True
            except sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError:
                session.rollback()
                return False #Email already exists
        return False

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    brand = Column(String)
    model = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., "Mountain", "Road", "Hybrid"
    frame_material = Column(String)
    wheel_size = Column(Integer)
    color = Column(String)
    gender = Column(String) # "Male", "Female", "Unisex"
    year = Column(Integer)

    images = relationship("BicycleImage", back_populates="bicycle")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="bicycle")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', brand='{self.brand}', model='{self.model}', price={self.price})>"

class BicycleImage(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycle_images'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'))
    image_url = Column(String)

    bicycle = relationship("Bicycle", back_populates="images")

class Part(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., "Tires", "Brakes", "Gears"
    brand = Column(String)
    weight = Column(Float)
    compatible_bicycles = Column(String) #Store compatible bicycle models as comma separated string

    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="part")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Part(name='{self.name}', category='{self.category}', price={self.price})>"

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    category = Column(String)  # e.g., "Helmets", "Lights", "Locks"
    brand = Column(String)
    color = Column(String)
    size = Column(String)

    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="accessory")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', category='{self.category}', price={self.price})>"

class Customer(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'customers'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    first_name = Column(String)
    last_name = Column(String)
    email = Column(String)
    phone_number = Column(String)
    address = Column(String)
    city = Column(String)
    state = Column(String)
    zip_code = Column(String)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="customer")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Customer(first_name='{self.first_name}', last_name='{self.last_name}', email='{self.email}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    customer_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('customers.id'))
    order_date = Column(String) #Store as string for simplicity
    shipping_address = Column(String)
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String) # e.g., "Pending", "Shipped", "Delivered"

    customer = relationship("Customer", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(customer_id={self.customer_id}, order_date='{self.order_date}', total_amount={self.total_amount})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'), nullable=True)
    part_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('parts.id'), nullable=True)
    accessory_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('accessories.id'), nullable=True)
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    price = Column(Float) #Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    bicycle = relationship("Bicycle", back_populates="order_items")
    part = relationship("Part", back_populates="order_items")
    accessory = relationship("Accessory", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, quantity={self.quantity}, price={self.price})>"

class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)

    def open_session(self):
        return self.Session()

    def close_session(self, session):
        session.close()

    # CRUD operations for Bicycles
    def add_bicycle(self, session, name, brand, model, description, price, quantity, category, frame_material, wheel_size, color, gender, year):
        bicycle = Bicycle(name=name, brand=brand, model=model, description=description, price=price, quantity=quantity, category=category, frame_material=frame_material, wheel_size=wheel_size, color=color, gender=gender, year=year)
        session.add(bicycle)
        session.commit()
        return bicycle

    def get_bicycle(self, session, bicycle_id):
        return session.query(Bicycle).get(bicycle_id)

    def update_bicycle(self, session, bicycle_id, **kwargs):
        bicycle = self.get_bicycle(session, bicycle_id)
        if bicycle:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(bicycle, key, value)
            session.commit()
            return bicycle
        return None

    def delete_bicycle(self, session, bicycle_id):
        bicycle = self.get_bicycle(session, bicycle_id)
        if bicycle:
            session.delete(bicycle)
            session.commit()
            return True
        return False

    def search_bicycles(self, session, search_term):
        search_term = f"%{search_term}%"
        return session.query(Bicycle).filter(
            sqlalchemy.or_(
                Bicycle.name.like(search_term),
                Bicycle.brand.like(search_term),
                Bicycle.model.like(search_term),
                Bicycle.description.like(search_term)
            )
        ).all()

    def filter_bicycles(self, session, **filters):
        query = session.query(Bicycle)
        for key, value in filters.items():
            if hasattr(Bicycle, key):
                query = query.filter(getattr(Bicycle, key) == value)
        return query.all()

    # CRUD operations for Parts
    def add_part(self, session, name, description, price, quantity, category, brand, weight, compatible_bicycles):
        part = Part(name=name, description=description, price=price, quantity=quantity, category=category, brand=brand, weight=weight, compatible_bicycles=compatible_bicycles)
        session.add(part)
        session.commit()
        return part

    def get_part(self, session, part_id):
        return session.query(Part).get(part_id)

    def update_part(self, session, part_id, **kwargs):
        part = self.get_part(session, part_id)
        if part:
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(part, key, value)
            session.commit()
            return part
        return None

    def delete_part(self, session, part_id):
        part = self.get_part(session, part_id)
        if part:
            session.delete(part)
            session.commit()
            return True
        return False

    def search_parts(self, session, search_term):
        search_term = f"%{search_term}%"
        return session.query(Part).filter(
            sqlalchemy.or_(
