        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, customer_id={self.customer_id}, date={self.order_date})>"


class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'), nullable=False)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'), nullable=True)
    part_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('parts.id'), nullable=True)
    accessory_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('accessories.id'), nullable=True)
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    unit_price = Column(Float, nullable=False)

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    bicycle = relationship("Bicycle", back_populates="orders")
    part = relationship("Part", back_populates="orders")
    accessory = relationship("Accessory", back_populates="orders")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, quantity={self.quantity}, unit_price={self.unit_price})>"


# Database Connection and Session Management
class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)  # Create tables if they don't exist
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)
        self.session = None

    def connect(self):
        """Establishes a new database session."""
        if self.session is None:
            self.session = self.Session()
        return self.session

    def close(self):
        """Closes the current database session."""
        if self.session:
            self.session.close()
            self.session = None

    def add_object(self, obj):
        """Adds a new object to the database."""
        self.session.add(obj)
        self.session.commit()

    def delete_object(self, obj):
        """Deletes an object from the database."""
        self.session.delete(obj)
        self.session.commit()

    def update_object(self, obj):
        """Updates an existing object in the database."""
        self.session.commit()

    def get_bicycle_by_id(self, bicycle_id):
        """Retrieves a bicycle by its ID."""
        return self.session.query(Bicycle).filter_by(id=bicycle_id).first()

    def get_part_by_id(self, part_id):
        """Retrieves a part by its ID."""
        return self.session.query(Part).filter_by(id=part_id).first()

    def get_accessory_by_id(self, accessory_id):
        """Retrieves an accessory by its ID."""
        return self.session.query(Accessory).filter_by(id=accessory_id).first()

    def get_customer_by_id(self, customer_id):
        """Retrieves a customer by their ID."""
        return self.session.query(Customer).filter_by(id=customer_id).first()

    def get_order_by_id(self, order_id):
        """Retrieves an order by its ID."""
        return self.session.query(Order).filter_by(id=order_id).first()

    def search_bicycles(self, query):
        """Searches for bicycles based on a query string (name or description)."""
        return self.session.query(Bicycle).filter(
            sqlalchemy.or_(
                Bicycle.name.like(f"%{query}%"),
                Bicycle.description.like(f"%{query}%")
            )
        ).all()

    def search_parts(self, query):
        """Searches for parts based on a query string (name or description)."""
        return self.session.query(Part).filter(
            sqlalchemy.or_(
                Part.name.like(f"%{query}%"),
                Part.description.like(f"%{query}%")
            )
        ).all()

    def search_accessories(self, query):
        """Searches for accessories based on a query string (name or description)."""
        return self.session.query(Accessory).filter(
            sqlalchemy.or_(
                Accessory.name.like(f"%{query}%"),
                Accessory.description.like(f"%{query}%")
            )
        ).all()

    def get_all_bicycles(self):
        """Retrieves all bicycles from the database."""
        return self.session.query(Bicycle).all()

    def get_all_parts(self):
        """Retrieves all parts from the database."""
        return self.session.query(Part).all()

    def get_all_accessories(self):
        """Retrieves all accessories from the database."""
        return self.session.query(Accessory).all()

    def get_all_customers(self):
        """Retrieves all customers from the database."""
        return self.session.query(Customer).all()

    def get_all_orders(self):
        """Retrieves all orders from the database."""
        return self.session.query(Order).all()

    def filter_bicycles_by_category(self, category):
        """Filters bicycles by category."""
        return self.session.query(Bicycle).filter_by(category=category).all()

    def filter_parts_by_category(self, category):
        """Filters parts by category."""
        return self.session.query(Part).filter_by(category=category).all()

    def filter_accessories_by_category(self, category):
        """Filters accessories by category."""
        return self.session.query(Accessory).filter_by(category=category).all()

    def create_order(self, customer_id, order_items):
        """Creates a new order."""
        customer = self.get_customer_by_id(customer_id)
        if not customer:
            raise ValueError("Customer not found.")

        order = Order(customer=customer)
        total_amount = 0.0

        for item in order_items:
            item_type = item.get('type')
            item_id = item.get('id')
            quantity = item.get('quantity')

            if item_type == 'bicycle':
                bicycle = self.get_bicycle_by_id(item_id)
                if not bicycle:
                    raise ValueError(f"Bicycle with id {item_id} not found.")
                if bicycle.stock_quantity < quantity:
                    raise ValueError(f"Not enough stock for bicycle {bicycle.name}.")

                unit_price = bicycle.price
                bicycle.stock_quantity -= quantity
                order_item = OrderItem(order=order, bicycle=bicycle, quantity=quantity, unit_price=unit_price)
                total_amount += unit_price * quantity

            elif item_type == 'part':
                part = self.get_part_by_id(item_id)
                if not part:
                    raise ValueError(f"Part with id {item_id} not found.")
                if part.stock_quantity < quantity:
                    raise ValueError(f"Not enough stock for part {part.name}.")

                unit_price = part.price
                part.stock_quantity -= quantity
                order_item = OrderItem(order=order, part=part, quantity=quantity, unit_price=unit_price)
                total_amount += unit_price * quantity

            elif item_type == 'accessory':
                accessory = self.get_accessory_by_id(item_id)
                if not accessory:
                    raise ValueError(f"Accessory with id {item_id} not found.")
                if accessory.stock_quantity < quantity:
                    raise ValueError(f"Not enough stock for accessory {accessory.name}.")

                unit_price = accessory.price
                accessory.stock_quantity -= quantity
                order_item = OrderItem(order=order, accessory=accessory, quantity=quantity, unit_price=unit_price)
                total_amount += unit_price * quantity
            else:
                raise ValueError("Invalid item type.")

            self.session.add(order_item)

        order.total_amount = total_amount
        self.session.add(order)
        self.session.commit()
        return order

        db_manager.add_object(customer1)
        db_manager.add_object(bicycle1)
        db_manager.add_object(part1)
        db_manager.add_object(accessory1)

        # Create a BicyclePart relationship
        bicycle_part1 = BicyclePart(bicycle=bicycle1, part=part1, quantity=2)
        db_manager.add_object(bicycle_part1)

        # Search for bicycles
        search_results = db_manager.search_bicycles('Mountain')
        print("Search Results:", search_results)

        # Filter bicycles by category
        mountain_bikes = db_manager.filter_bicycles_by_category('Mountain')
        print("Mountain Bikes:", mountain_bikes)

        # Create an order
        order_items = [
            {'type': 'bicycle', 'id': bicycle1.id, 'quantity': 1},
            {'type': 'part', 'id': part1.id, 'quantity': 2},
            {'type': 'accessory', 'id': accessory1.id, 'quantity': 1}
        ]
        order = db_manager.create_order(customer1.id, order_items)

    except Exception as e:
        print(f"An error occurred: {e}")
        if db_manager.session:
            db_manager.session.rollback()  # Rollback changes in case of error

    finally:
        db_manager.close()

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, DateTime, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
import datetime

Base = declarative_base()

class Bicycle(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycles'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(1000))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, default=0)
    category = Column(String(50))  # e.g., "Mountain", "Road", "Hybrid"
    image_url = Column(String(255)) # URL to the bicycle's image

    parts = relationship("BicyclePart", back_populates="bicycle")
    orders = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="bicycle")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Bicycle(name='{self.name}', price={self.price}, stock={self.stock_quantity})>"


class Part(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'parts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(1000))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, default=0)
    category = Column(String(50)) # e.g., "Brakes", "Tires", "Gears"
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    bicycle_parts = relationship("BicyclePart", back_populates="part")
    orders = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="part")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Part(name='{self.name}', price={self.price}, stock={self.stock_quantity})>"


class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(1000))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, default=0)
    category = Column(String(50)) # e.g., "Helmets", "Lights", "Locks"
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    orders = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="accessory")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', price={self.price}, stock={self.stock_quantity})>"


class BicyclePart(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'bicycle_parts'

    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'), primary_key=True)
    part_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('parts.id'), primary_key=True)
    quantity = Column(Integer, default=1)

    bicycle = relationship("Bicycle", back_populates="parts")
    part = relationship("Part", back_populates="bicycle_parts")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<BicyclePart(bicycle_id={self.bicycle_id}, part_id={self.part_id}, quantity={self.quantity})>"


class Customer(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'customers'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    first_name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    last_name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    email = Column(String(255), unique=True, nullable=False)
    phone_number = Column(String(20))
    address = Column(String(255))

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="customer")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Customer(name='{self.first_name} {self.last_name}', email='{self.email}')>"


class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    customer_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('customers.id'), nullable=False)
    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.datetime.utcnow)
    total_amount = Column(Float, default=0.0)
    status = Column(String(50), default="Pending") # e.g., "Pending", "Shipped", "Delivered", "Cancelled"

    customer = relationship("Customer", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, customer_id={self.customer_id}, date={self.order_date})>"


class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'), nullable=False)
    bicycle_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('bicycles.id'), nullable=True)
    part_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('parts.id'), nullable=True)
    accessory_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('accessories.id'), nullable=True)

    bicycle = relationship("Bicycle", back_populates="orders")

    accessory = relationship("Accessory", back_populates="orders")

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, DateTime, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship, sessionmaker
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
import datetime

Base = declarative_base()

class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False, unique=True)
    description = Column(String(1000))

    products = relationship("Product", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"

class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'products'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(1000))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="products")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="product")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Product(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False)
    password = Column(String(255), nullable=False)  # Store hashed passwords
    email = Column(String(255), unique=True, nullable=False)
    first_name = Column(String(255))
    last_name = Column(String(255))
    address = Column(String(255))
    city = Column(String(255))
    state = Column(String(255))
    zip_code = Column(String(20))

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.datetime.utcnow)
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String(50), default='Pending') # e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, user_id={self.user_id}, total={self.total_amount})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, DateTime, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship, sessionmaker
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
import datetime

Base = declarative_base()

class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False, unique=True)
    description = Column(String(1000))

    products = relationship("Product", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"

class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'products'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(1000))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    image_url = Column(String(255))
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, default=0)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))

    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="products")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="product")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Product(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class Customer(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'customers'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    first_name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    last_name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    email = Column(String(255), nullable=False, unique=True)
    phone_number = Column(String(20))
    address = Column(String(255))

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="customer")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Customer(first_name='{self.first_name}', last_name='{self.last_name}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    customer_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('customers.id'))
    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.datetime.utcnow)
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String(50), default='Pending') # e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled

    customer = relationship("Customer", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, customer_id={self.customer_id}, order_date={self.order_date})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False) # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    product = relationship("Product", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, product_id={self.product_id}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

class ShippingAddress(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'shipping_addresses'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    address_line1 = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    address_line2 = Column(String(255))
    city = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    state = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    zip_code = Column(String(20), nullable=False)
    country = Column(String(255), nullable=False)

    order = relationship("Order") # One-to-one relationship with Order

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<ShippingAddress(order_id={self.order_id}, city='{self.city}')>"

def create_database(db_url):
    engine = create_engine(db_url)
    Base.metadata.create_all(engine)
    return engine

def create_session(engine):
    Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
    return Session()

def add_category(session, name, description=None):
    category = Category(name=name, description=description)
    session.add(category)
    session.commit()
    return category

def get_category_by_name(session, name):
    return session.query(Category).filter_by(name=name).first()

def add_product(session, name, description, price, category_name, image_url=None, stock_quantity=0):
    category = get_category_by_name(session, category_name)
    if not category:
        raise ValueError(f"Category '{category_name}' not found.")

    product = Product(name=name, description=description, price=price, image_url=image_url, stock_quantity=stock_quantity, category=category)
    session.add(product)
    session.commit()
    return product

def get_product_by_name(session, name):
    return session.query(Product).filter_by(name=name).first()

def update_product_price(session, product_name, new_price):
    product = get_product_by_name(session, product_name)
    if product:
        product.price = new_price
        session.commit()
        return True
    return False

def delete_product(session, product_name):
    product = get_product_by_name(session, product_name)
    if product:
        session.delete(product)
        session.commit()
        return True
    return False

def filter_products_by_price_range(session, min_price, max_price):
    return session.query(Product).filter(Product.price >= min_price, Product.price <= max_price).all()

def add_customer(session, first_name, last_name, email, phone_number=None, address=None):
    customer = Customer(first_name=first_name, last_name=last_name, email=email, phone_number=phone_number, address=address)
    session.add(customer)
    session.commit()
    return customer

def get_customer_by_email(session, email):
    return session.query(Customer).filter_by(email=email).first()

def add_order(session, customer_email, order_items_data):
    """
    Adds an order to the database.

    Args:
        session: SQLAlchemy session.
        customer_email: Email of the customer placing the order.
        order_items_data: A list of dictionaries, where each dictionary represents an order item
                         and contains 'product_name' and 'quantity'.  Example:
                         [{'product_name': 'Mountain Bike', 'quantity': 1}, {'product_name': 'Helmet', 'quantity': 2}]

    Returns:
        The newly created Order object.
    """
    customer = get_customer_by_email(session, customer_email)
    if not customer:
        raise ValueError(f"Customer with email '{customer_email}' not found.")

    order = Order(customer=customer)
    session.add(order)
    session.flush()  # Assign an ID to the order

    total_amount = 0.0
    for item_data in order_items_data:
        product_name = item_data['product_name']
        quantity = item_data['quantity']
        product = get_product_by_name(session, product_name)

        if not product:
            session.rollback()
            raise ValueError(f"Product '{product_name}' not found.")

        if product.stock_quantity < quantity:
            session.rollback()
            raise ValueError(f"Not enough stock for product '{product_name}'. Available: {product.stock_quantity}, Requested: {quantity}")

        order_item = OrderItem(order=order, product=product, quantity=quantity, price=product.price)
        session.add(order_item)
        total_amount += product.price * quantity
        product.stock_quantity -= quantity # Update stock

    order.total_amount = total_amount
    session.commit()
    return order

def get_order_by_id(session, order_id):
    return session.query(Order).filter_by(id=order_id).first()

def update_order_status(session, order_id, new_status):
    order = get_order_by_id(session, order_id)
    if order:
        order.status = new_status
        session.commit()
        return True
    return False

def add_shipping_address(session, order_id, address_line1, address_line2, city, state, zip_code, country):
    order = get_order_by_id(session, order_id)
    if not order:
        raise ValueError(f"Order with id '{order_id}' not found.")

    shipping_address = ShippingAddress(order_id=order_id, address_line1=address_line1, address_line2=address_line2, city=city, state=state, zip_code=zip_code, country=country)
    session.add(shipping_address)
    session.commit()
    return shipping_address

if __name__ == '__main__':
    db_url = 'sqlite:///./bike_store.db'  # Use a relative path
    engine = create_database(db_url)
    session = create_session(engine)

    # Example Usage:

    # Add Categories
    try:
        road_bikes = add_category(session, "Road Bikes", "Bikes designed for paved roads.")
        mountain_bikes = add_category(session, "Mountain Bikes", "Bikes designed for off-road trails.")
        helmets = add_category(session, "Helmets", "Protective headgear for cycling.")
        print("Categories added successfully.")
    except sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError:
        print("Categories already exist.")
        session.rollback()

    # Add Products
    try:
        add_product(session, "Enduro 29", "Full suspension mountain bike", 3500.00, "Mountain Bikes", stock_quantity=5)
        add_product(session, "Allez Sprint", "Aluminum road bike", 2200.00, "Road Bikes", stock_quantity=10)
        add_product(session, "Propero III", "High-performance road helmet", 150.00, "Helmets", stock_quantity=20)
        print("Products added successfully.")
    except ValueError as e:
        print(f"Error adding products: {e}")

    # Add Customer
    try:
        john_doe = add_customer(session, "John", "Doe", "john.doe@example.com", "555-123-4567", "123 Main St")
        print("Customer added successfully.")
    except sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError:
        print("Customer already exists.")
        session.rollback()

    # Create an Order
    try:
        order_items = [
            {'product_name': 'Enduro 29', 'quantity': 1},
            {'product_name': 'Propero III', 'quantity': 1}
        ]
        order = add_order(session, "john.doe@example.com", order_items)
        print(f"Order created successfully with ID: {order.id}")
    except ValueError as e:
        print(f"Error creating order: {e}")

    # Add Shipping Address
    try:
        if order:
            add_shipping_address(session, order.id, "123 Shipping St", None, "Anytown", "CA", "91234", "USA")
            print("Shipping address added successfully.")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error adding shipping address: {e}")

    # Example Queries
    road_bike_category = get_category_by_name(session, "Road Bikes")
    print(f"Road Bike Category: {road_bike_category}")

    allez_sprint = get_product_by_name(session, "Allez Sprint")
    print(f"Allez Sprint Product: {allez_sprint}")

    products_in_price_range = filter_products_by_price_range(session, 100, 5000)
    print("Products in price range $100-$5000:")
    for product in products_in_price_range:
        print(f"- {product.name}: ${product.price}")

    # Update Order Status
    if order:
        update_order_status(session, order.id, "Shipped")
        updated_order = get_order_by_id(session, order.id)
        print(f"Order status updated to: {updated_order.status}")

    # Clean up (optional - remove data)
    # session.delete(road_bikes)
    # session.delete(mountain_bikes)
    # session.delete(helmets)
    # session.delete(john_doe)
    # session.commit()

    session.close()

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, DateTime, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship, sessionmaker
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
import datetime

Base = declarative_base()

class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False, unique=True)
    description = Column(String(1000))

    products = relationship("Product", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"

class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'products'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(1000))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))
    image_url = Column(String(255))

    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="products")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="product")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Product(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False)
    password = Column(String(255), nullable=False)  # Store hashed passwords
    email = Column(String(255), unique=True, nullable=False)
    first_name = Column(String(255))
    last_name = Column(String(255))
    address = Column(String(255))
    city = Column(String(255))
    state = Column(String(255))
    zip_code = Column(String(20))

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.datetime.utcnow)
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String(50), default='Pending') # e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, user_id={self.user_id}, total={self.total_amount})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)  # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    product = relationship("Product", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, product_id={self.product_id}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

class ShoppingCart(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'shopping_carts'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.id'))
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    added_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.datetime.utcnow)

    user = relationship("User")
    product = relationship("Product")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<ShoppingCart(user_id={self.user_id}, product_id={self.product_id}, quantity={self.quantity})>"

def create_database(db_url):
    engine = create_engine(db_url)
    Base.metadata.create_all(engine)
    return engine

def create_session(engine):
    Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
    return Session()

def add_category(session, name, description=None):
    category = Category(name=name, description=description)
    session.add(category)
    session.commit()
    return category

def get_category_by_name(session, name):
    return session.query(Category).filter_by(name=name).first()

def add_product(session, name, description, price, stock_quantity, category, image_url=None):
    product = Product(name=name, description=description, price=price, stock_quantity=stock_quantity, category=category, image_url=image_url)
    session.add(product)
    session.commit()
    return product

def get_product_by_name(session, name):
    return session.query(Product).filter_by(name=name).first()

def update_product_stock(session, product_id, new_quantity):
    product = session.query(Product).get(product_id)
    if product:
        product.stock_quantity = new_quantity
        session.commit()
        return True
    return False

def delete_product(session, product_id):
    product = session.query(Product).get(product_id)
    if product:
        session.delete(product)
        session.commit()
        return True
    return False

def filter_products_by_category(session, category_name):
    category = get_category_by_name(session, category_name)
    if category:
        return category.products
    return []

def add_user(session, username, password, email, first_name=None, last_name=None, address=None, city=None, state=None, zip_code=None):
    user = User(username=username, password=password, email=email, first_name=first_name, last_name=last_name, address=address, city=city, state=state, zip_code=zip_code)
    session.add(user)
    session.commit()
    return user

def get_user_by_username(session, username):
    return session.query(User).filter_by(username=username).first()

def create_order(session, user, total_amount):
    order = Order(user=user, total_amount=total_amount)
    session.add(order)
    session.commit()
    return order

def add_order_item(session, order, product, quantity, price):
    order_item = OrderItem(order=order, product=product, quantity=quantity, price=price)
    session.add(order_item)
    session.commit()
    return order_item

def get_order_by_id(session, order_id):
    return session.query(Order).get(order_id)

def add_to_cart(session, user, product, quantity):
    cart_item = ShoppingCart(user=user, product=product, quantity=quantity)
    session.add(cart_item)
    session.commit()
    return cart_item

def get_cart_items_by_user(session, user):
    return session.query(ShoppingCart).filter_by(user=user).all()

def remove_from_cart(session, cart_item_id):
    cart_item = session.query(ShoppingCart).get(cart_item_id)
    if cart_item:
        session.delete(cart_item)
        session.commit()
        return True
    return False

    db_url = 'sqlite:///./bike_store.db'  # Use an in-memory SQLite database for testing
    engine = create_database(db_url)
    Session = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
    session = Session()

    # Example Usage
    try:
        # Create Categories
        bikes_category = add_category(session, "Bikes", "Complete bicycles")
        parts_category = add_category(session, "Parts", "Individual bike components")
        accessories_category = add_category(session, "Accessories", "Bike-related accessories")

        # Create Products
        product1 = add_product(session, "Mountain Bike", "Full suspension mountain bike", 1500.00, 10, bikes_category)
        product2 = add_product(session, "Road Bike", "Lightweight road bike", 1200.00, 5, bikes_category)
        product3 = add_product(session, "Bike Helmet", "High-impact bike helmet", 50.00, 50, accessories_category)
        product4 = add_product(session, "Shimano Derailleur", "High-quality Shimano derailleur", 75.00, 20, parts_category)

        # Create a User
        user1 = add_user(session, "john_doe", "password123", "john.doe@example.com", "John", "Doe")

        # Create an Order
        order1 = create_order(session, user1, 1550.00)  # Mountain Bike + Helmet

        # Add Order Items
        add_order_item(session, order1, product1, 1, 1500.00)
        add_order_item(session, order1, product3, 1, 50.00)

        # Add items to shopping cart
        add_to_cart(session, user1, product2, 1)
        add_to_cart(session, user1, product4, 2)

        # Retrieve and print information
        print("Categories:", session.query(Category).all())
        print("Products:", session.query(Product).all())
        print("User:", get_user_by_username(session, "john_doe"))
        print("Order:", get_order_by_id(session, order1.id))
        print("Cart items:", get_cart_items_by_user(session, user1))

        # Filter products by category
        mountain_bikes = filter_products_by_category(session, "Bikes")
        print("Mountain Bikes:", mountain_bikes)

        # Update product stock
        update_product_stock(session, product1.id, 8)
        print("Updated Mountain Bike stock:", get_product_by_name(session, "Mountain Bike"))

        # Remove an item from the cart
        cart_items = get_cart_items_by_user(session, user1)
        if cart_items:
            remove_from_cart(session, cart_items[0].id)
            print("Cart items after removal:", get_cart_items_by_user(session, user1))

    except Exception as e:
        print(f"An error occurred: {e}")
        session.rollback()  # Rollback changes in case of error
    finally:
        session.close()

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, DateTime, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
import datetime

Base = declarative_base()

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False)
    email = Column(String(120), unique=True, nullable=False)
    password = Column(String(60), nullable=False)  # Store password hashes, not plain text
    is_admin = Column(Boolean, default=False)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}', email='{self.email}')>"


class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(255))

    products = relationship("Product", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"


class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'products'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(255))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    image_url = Column(String(255))
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, default=0)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))

    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="products")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="product")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Product(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"


class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=False)
    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.datetime.utcnow)
    total_amount = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    status = Column(String(50), default='Pending')  # e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(order_date='{self.order_date}', total_amount={self.total_amount})>"


class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'), nullable=False)
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.id'), nullable=False)
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)  # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    product = relationship("Product", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(quantity={self.quantity}, price={self.price})>"


class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)

    def create_database(self):
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)

    def get_session(self):
        return self.Session()

    def add_object(self, obj):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            session.add(obj)
            session.commit()
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def delete_object(self, obj):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            session.delete(obj)
            session.commit()
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def update_object(self, obj):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            session.merge(obj)
            session.commit()
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def filter_objects(self, model, **kwargs):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            return session.query(model).filter_by(**kwargs).all()
        except Exception as e:
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def search_objects(self, model, search_term, *fields):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            filters = []
            for field in fields:
                filters.append(field.like(f"%{search_term}%"))
            return session.query(model).filter(sqlalchemy.or_(*filters)).all()
        except Exception as e:
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def get_all_objects(self, model):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            return session.query(model).all()
        except Exception as e:
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Example Usage
    db_url = 'sqlite:///./cycle_workshop.db'  # Use an in-memory database for testing
    db_manager = DatabaseManager(db_url)
    db_manager.create_database()

    # Create some categories
    category1 = Category(name='Bikes', description='Complete bicycles')
    category2 = Category(name='Wheels', description='Bicycle wheels and wheelsets')
    category3 = Category(name='Accessories', description='Bicycle accessories')

    db_manager.add_object(category1)
    db_manager.add_object(category2)
    db_manager.add_object(category3)

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, DateTime, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
import datetime

Base = declarative_base()

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False)
    email = Column(String(120), unique=True, nullable=False)
    password = Column(String(60), nullable=False)  # Store password hashes, not plain text
    is_admin = Column(Boolean, default=False)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}', email='{self.email}')>"


class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(255))

    products = relationship("Product", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"


class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'products'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(255))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    image_url = Column(String(255))
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, default=0)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))

    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="products")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="product")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Product(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"


class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=False)
    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.datetime.utcnow)
    total_amount = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    status = Column(String(50), default='Pending')  # e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(order_date='{self.order_date}', total_amount={self.total_amount})>"


class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'), nullable=False)
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.id'), nullable=False)
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)  # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    product = relationship("Product", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(quantity={self.quantity}, price={self.price})>"


class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)

    def create_database(self):
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)

    def get_session(self):
        return self.Session()

    def add_object(self, obj):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            session.add(obj)
            session.commit()
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def delete_object(self, obj):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            session.delete(obj)
            session.commit()
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def update_object(self, obj):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            session.merge(obj)
            session.commit()
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def filter_objects(self, model, **kwargs):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            return session.query(model).filter_by(**kwargs).all()
        except Exception as e:
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def search_objects(self, model, search_term, *fields):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            filters = []
            for field in fields:
                filters.append(field.like(f"%{search_term}%"))
            return session.query(model).filter(sqlalchemy.or_(*filters)).all()
        except Exception as e:
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def get_all_objects(self, model):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            return session.query(model).all()
        except Exception as e:
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, DateTime, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
import datetime

Base = declarative_base()

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False)
    email = Column(String(120), unique=True, nullable=False)
    password = Column(String(60), nullable=False)  # Store password hashes, not plain text
    is_admin = Column(Boolean, default=False)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}', email='{self.email}')>"


class Category(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'categories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(50), unique=True, nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(255))

    products = relationship("Product", back_populates="category")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Category(name='{self.name}')>"


class Product(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'products'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    description = Column(String(255))
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    image_url = Column(String(255))
    stock_quantity = Column(Integer, default=0)
    category_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('categories.id'))

    category = relationship("Category", back_populates="products")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="product")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Product(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"


class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=False)
    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.datetime.utcnow)
    total_amount = Column(Float, default=0.0)
    status = Column(String(50), default='Pending')  # e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, user_id={self.user_id}, order_date={self.order_date})>"


class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'), nullable=False)
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.id'), nullable=False)
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)  # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    product = relationship("Product", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, product_id={self.product_id}, quantity={self.quantity})>"


class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)

    def create_database(self):
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)

    def get_session(self):
        return self.Session()

    def add_object(self, obj):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            session.add(obj)
            session.commit()
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def add_multiple_objects(self, objects):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            session.add_all(objects)
            session.commit()
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def delete_object(self, obj):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            session.delete(obj)
            session.commit()
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def update_object(self, obj):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            session.merge(obj)  # Use merge to update detached objects
            session.commit()
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def get_all(self, model):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            return session.query(model).all()
        except Exception as e:
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def get_by_id(self, model, id):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            return session.query(model).get(id)
        except Exception as e:
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def filter_objects(self, model, filter_criteria):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            return session.query(model).filter(filter_criteria).all()
        except Exception as e:
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def search_objects(self, model, search_term, *fields):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            filters = []
            for field in fields:
                filters.append(field.like(f"%{search_term}%"))
            return session.query(model).filter(sqlalchemy.or_(*filters)).all()
        except Exception as e:
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Example Usage
    db_url = 'sqlite:///./cycle_workshop.db'  # Use an absolute path for better reliability
    db_manager = DatabaseManager(db_url)
    db_manager.create_database()

    # Create some sample data
    category1 = Category(name='Bikes', description='Complete bicycles')
    category2 = Category(name='Parts', description='Spare parts for bicycles')

    product1 = Product(name='Mountain Bike', description='Full suspension mountain bike', price=1500.00, stock_quantity=10, category=category1)
    product2 = Product(name='Brake Pads', description='Replacement brake pads', price=25.00, stock_quantity=100, category=category2)

    user1 = User(username='john_doe', email='john.doe@example.com', password='password123') # In real app, hash the password!

    order1 = Order(user=user1)
    order_item1 = OrderItem(order=order1, product=product1, quantity=1, price=product1.price)
    order_item2 = OrderItem(order=order1, product=product2, quantity=2, price=product2.price)

    # Add data to the database
    db_manager.add_multiple_objects([category1, category2, product1, product2, user1, order1, order_item1, order_item2])

    # Retrieve all products
    all_products = db_manager.get_all(Product)
    print("All Products:", all_products)

    # Retrieve a product by ID
    product_id_1 = db_manager.get_by_id(Product, 1)
    print("Product with ID 1:", product_id_1)

    # Filter products by category
    bikes = db_manager.filter_objects(Product, Product.category_id == category1.id)
    print("Bikes:", bikes)

    # Search for products by name
    search_results = db_manager.search_objects(Product, 'Bike', Product.name, Product.description)
    print("Search Results:", search_results)

    # Update a product's stock quantity
    product1.stock_quantity = 8
    db_manager.update_object(product1)
    updated_product = db_manager.get_by_id(Product, 1)
    print("Updated Product Stock:", updated_product.stock_quantity)

    order_date = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.datetime.utcnow)
    total_amount = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    status = Column(String(50), default='Pending')  # e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(order_date='{self.order_date}', total_amount={self.total_amount})>"


class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'), nullable=False)
    product_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('products.id'), nullable=False)
    quantity = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    price = Column(Float, nullable=False)  # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    product = relationship("Product", back_populates="order_items")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(quantity={self.quantity}, price={self.price})>"


class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)

    def create_database(self):
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)

    def get_session(self):
        return self.Session()

    def add_object(self, obj):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            session.add(obj)
            session.commit()
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def delete_object(self, obj):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            session.delete(obj)
            session.commit()
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def update_object(self, obj):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            session.merge(obj)
            session.commit()
        except Exception as e:
            session.rollback()
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def filter_objects(self, model, **kwargs):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            return session.query(model).filter_by(**kwargs).all()
        except Exception as e:
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def search_objects(self, model, search_term, *fields):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            filters = []
            for field in fields:
                filters.append(field.like(f"%{search_term}%"))
            return session.query(model).filter(sqlalchemy.or_(*filters)).all()
        except Exception as e:
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

    def get_all_objects(self, model):
        session = self.get_session()
        try:
            return session.query(model).all()
        except Exception as e:
            raise e
        finally:
            session.close()

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Wheel(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'wheels'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    size = Column(Integer)  # e.g., diameter in inches
    material = Column(String) # e.g., Aluminum, Carbon Fiber
    tire_type = Column(String) # e.g., Clincher, Tubeless
    brand = Column(String)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)

    images = relationship("WheelImage", back_populates="wheel")
    reviews = relationship("Review", back_populates="wheel")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="wheel")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Wheel(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class WheelImage(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'wheel_images'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    wheel_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('wheels.id'))
    image_url = Column(String)

    wheel = relationship("Wheel", back_populates="images")

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    type = Column(String) # e.g., Tire levers, pumps, etc.
    brand = Column(String)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)

    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="accessory")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String)
    password = Column(String)  # Store hashed passwords in a real application
    email = Column(String)
    address = Column(String)
    is_admin = Column(Boolean, default=False)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")
    reviews = relationship("Review", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}', email='{self.email}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    order_date = Column(String) #Store as string for simplicity, use datetime for real app
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String) # e.g., "Pending", "Shipped", "Delivered"

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    wheel_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('wheels.id'), nullable=True)
    accessory_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('accessories.id'), nullable=True)
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    price = Column(Float) # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    wheel = relationship("Wheel", back_populates="order_items")

class Review(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'reviews'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)

    wheel_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('wheels.id'))

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="reviews")
    wheel = relationship("Wheel", back_populates="reviews")

class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)
        self.session = self.Session()

    def create_session(self):
        self.session = self.Session()
        return self.session

    def close_session(self):
        if self.session:
            self.session.close()

    # Generic add, delete, update
    def add(self, item):
        self.session.add(item)
        self.session.commit()

    def delete(self, item):
        self.session.delete(item)
        self.session.commit()

    def update(self, item):
        self.session.commit()

    # Wheel operations
    def add_wheel(self, name, description, price, size, material, tire_type, brand, quantity_in_stock):
        wheel = Wheel(name=name, description=description, price=price, size=size, material=material, tire_type=tire_type, brand=brand, quantity_in_stock=quantity_in_stock)
        self.add(wheel)
        return wheel

    def get_wheel_by_id(self, wheel_id):
        return self.session.query(Wheel).filter_by(id=wheel_id).first()

    def get_wheels_by_brand(self, brand):
        return self.session.query(Wheel).filter_by(brand=brand).all()

    def get_wheels_by_size(self, size):
        return self.session.query(Wheel).filter_by(size=size).all()

    def update_wheel_quantity(self, wheel_id, new_quantity):
        wheel = self.get_wheel_by_id(wheel_id)
        if wheel:
            wheel.quantity_in_stock = new_quantity
            self.update(wheel)
            return True
        return False

    def search_wheels(self, query):
        return self.session.query(Wheel).filter(Wheel.name.contains(query) | Wheel.description.contains(query) | Wheel.brand.contains(query)).all()

    # Accessory operations
    def add_accessory(self, name, description, price, type, brand, quantity_in_stock):
        accessory = Accessory(name=name, description=description, price=price, type=type, brand=brand, quantity_in_stock=quantity_in_stock)
        self.add(accessory)
        return accessory

    def get_accessory_by_id(self, accessory_id):
        return self.session.query(Accessory).filter_by(id=accessory_id).first()

    def get_accessories_by_type(self, accessory_type):
        return self.session.query(Accessory).filter_by(type=accessory_type).all()

    def update_accessory_quantity(self, accessory_id, new_quantity):
        accessory = self.get_accessory_by_id(accessory_id)
        if accessory:
            accessory.quantity_in_stock = new_quantity
            self.update(accessory)
            return True
        return False

    def search_accessories(self, query):
        return self.session.query(Accessory).filter(Accessory.name.contains(query) | Accessory.description.contains(query) | Accessory.brand.contains(query)).all()

    # User operations
    def add_user(self, username, password, email, address, is_admin=False):
        user = User(username=username, password=password, email=email, address=address, is_admin=is_admin)
        self.add(user)
        return user

    def get_user_by_username(self, username):
        return self.session.query(User).filter_by(username=username).first()

    def get_user_by_id(self, user_id):
        return self.session.query(User).filter_by(id=user_id).first()

    # Order operations
    def create_order(self, user_id, order_date, total_amount, status):
        order = Order(user_id=user_id, order_date=order_date, total_amount=total_amount, status=status)
        self.add(order)
        return order

    def get_order_by_id(self, order_id):
        return self.session.query(Order).filter_by(id=order_id).first()

    def get_orders_by_user(self, user_id):
        return self.session.query(Order).filter_by(user_id=user_id).all()

    # Order Item operations
    def add_order_item(self, order_id, wheel_id, accessory_id, quantity, price):
        order_item = OrderItem(order_id=order_id, wheel_id=wheel_id, accessory_id=accessory_id, quantity=quantity, price=price)
        self.add(order_item)
        return order_item

    # Review operations
    def add_review(self, user_id, wheel_id, rating, comment, review_date):
        review = Review(user_id=user_id, wheel_id=wheel_id, rating=rating, comment=comment, review_date=review_date)
        self.add(review)
        return review

    def get_reviews_by_wheel(self, wheel_id):
        return self.session.query(Review).filter_by(wheel_id=wheel_id).all()

    def get_review_by_id(self, review_id):
        return self.session.query(Review).filter_by(id=review_id).first()

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Wheel(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'wheels'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    size = Column(Integer)  # Wheel diameter in inches
    material = Column(String) # e.g., Aluminum, Carbon Fiber
    tire_type = Column(String) # e.g., Clincher, Tubeless
    brand = Column(String)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)
    image_url = Column(String) # URL to wheel image

    orders = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="wheel")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Wheel(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    type = Column(String) # e.g., Pump, Tire Lever, Multi-tool
    brand = Column(String)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)
    image_url = Column(String) # URL to accessory image

    orders = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="accessory")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String, unique=True, nullable=False)
    password = Column(String, nullable=False) # In real app, hash this!
    email = Column(String, unique=True)
    first_name = Column(String)
    last_name = Column(String)
    address = Column(String)
    city = Column(String)
    state = Column(String)
    zip_code = Column(String)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}', email='{self.email}')>"

class Order(Base):

    total_amount = Column(Float)
    shipping_address = Column(String)
    billing_address = Column(String)
    status = Column(String) # e.g., "Pending", "Shipped", "Delivered"

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Order(id={self.id}, user_id={self.user_id}, total_amount={self.total_amount})>"

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    wheel_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('wheels.id'), nullable=True) # Allow null if it's an accessory
    accessory_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('accessories.id'), nullable=True) # Allow null if it's a wheel
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    price = Column(Float) # Price at the time of order

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<OrderItem(order_id={self.order_id}, quantity={self.quantity}, price={self.price})>"

            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(order_item, key, value)
            self.session.commit()
            return True
        return False

    def delete_order_item(self, order_item_id):
        order_item = self.get_order_item_by_id(order_item_id)
        if order_item:
            self.session.delete(order_item)
            self.session.commit()
            return True
        return False

import sqlalchemy
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Wheel(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'wheels'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    size = Column(Integer)  # e.g., diameter in inches
    material = Column(String) # e.g., Aluminum, Carbon Fiber
    tire_type = Column(String) # e.g., Clincher, Tubeless
    brand = Column(String)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)

    images = relationship("WheelImage", back_populates="wheel")
    reviews = relationship("Review", back_populates="wheel")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="wheel")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Wheel(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class WheelImage(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'wheel_images'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    wheel_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('wheels.id'))
    image_url = Column(String)

    wheel = relationship("Wheel", back_populates="images")

class Accessory(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'accessories'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    type = Column(String) # e.g., Tire levers, pumps, etc.
    brand = Column(String)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)

    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="accessory")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Accessory(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'users'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    username = Column(String)
    password = Column(String)  # Store hashed passwords in a real application
    email = Column(String)
    address = Column(String)
    is_admin = Column(Boolean, default=False)

    orders = relationship("Order", back_populates="user")
    reviews = relationship("Review", back_populates="user")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<User(username='{self.username}', email='{self.email}')>"

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    order_date = Column(String) #Store as string for simplicity, use datetime for real app
    total_amount = Column(Float)
    status = Column(String) # e.g., "Pending", "Shipped", "Delivered"

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
    order_items = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="order")

class OrderItem(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'order_items'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    order_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('orders.id'))
    wheel_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('wheels.id'), nullable=True)
    accessory_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('accessories.id'), nullable=True)
    quantity = Column(Integer)
    price = Column(Float) # Price at the time of order

    order = relationship("Order", back_populates="order_items")
    wheel = relationship("Wheel", back_populates="order_items")
    accessory = relationship("Accessory", back_populates="order_items")

class Review(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'reviews'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'))
    wheel_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('wheels.id'))
    rating = Column(Integer) # e.g., 1-5 stars
    comment = Column(String)
    review_date = Column(String) #Store as string for simplicity, use datetime for real app

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="reviews")
    wheel = relationship("Wheel", back_populates="reviews")

class DatabaseManager:
    def __init__(self, db_url):
        self.engine = create_engine(db_url)
        Base.metadata.create_all(self.engine)
        self.Session = sessionmaker(bind=self.engine)
        self.session = self.Session()

    def create_session(self):
        self.session = self.Session()
        return self.session

    def close_session(self):
        if self.session:
            self.session.close()

    # Generic add, delete, update
    def add(self, item):
        self.session.add(item)
        self.session.commit()

    def delete(self, item):
        self.session.delete(item)
        self.session.commit()

    def update(self, item):
        self.session.commit()

    # Wheel operations
    def add_wheel(self, name, description, price, size, material, tire_type, brand, quantity_in_stock):
        wheel = Wheel(name=name, description=description, price=price, size=size, material=material, tire_type=tire_type, brand=brand, quantity_in_stock=quantity_in_stock)
        self.add(wheel)
        return wheel

    def get_wheel_by_id(self, wheel_id):
        return self.session.query(Wheel).filter_by(id=wheel_id).first()

    def get_wheels_by_brand(self, brand):
        return self.session.query(Wheel).filter_by(brand=brand).all()

    def get_wheels_by_size(self, size):
        return self.session.query(Wheel).filter_by(size=size).all()

    def update_wheel_quantity(self, wheel_id, new_quantity):
        wheel = self.get_wheel_by_id(wheel_id)
        if wheel:
            wheel.quantity_in_stock = new_quantity
            self.update(wheel)
            return True
        return False

    def search_wheels(self, query):
        return self.session.query(Wheel).filter(Wheel.name.contains(query) | Wheel.description.contains(query) | Wheel.brand.contains(query)).all()

    # Accessory operations
    def add_accessory(self, name, description, price, type, brand, quantity_in_stock):
        accessory = Accessory(name=name, description=description, price=price, type=type, brand=brand, quantity_in_stock=quantity_in_stock)
        self.add(accessory)
        return accessory

    def get_accessory_by_id(self, accessory_id):
        return self.session.query(Accessory).filter_by(id=accessory_id).first()

    def get_accessories_by_type(self, accessory_type):
        return self.session.query(Accessory).filter_by(type=accessory_type).all()

    def update_accessory_quantity(self, accessory_id, new_quantity):
        accessory = self.get_accessory_by_id(accessory_id)
        if accessory:
            accessory.quantity_in_stock = new_quantity
            self.update(accessory)
            return True
        return False

    def search_accessories(self, query):
        return self.session.query(Accessory).filter(Accessory.name.contains(query) | Accessory.description.contains(query) | Accessory.brand.contains(query)).all()

    # User operations
    def add_user(self, username, password, email, address, is_admin=False):
        user = User(username=username, password=password, email=email, address=address, is_admin=is_admin)
        self.add(user)
        return user

    def get_user_by_username(self, username):
        return self.session.query(User).filter_by(username=username).first()

    def get_user_by_id(self, user_id):
        return self.session.query(User).filter_by(id=user_id).first()

    # Order operations
    def create_order(self, user_id, order_date, total_amount, status):
        order = Order(user_id=user_id, order_date=order_date, total_amount=total_amount, status=status)
        self.add(order)
        return order

    def get_order_by_id(self, order_id):
        return self.session.query(Order).filter_by(id=order_id).first()

    def get_orders_by_user(self, user_id):
        return self.session.query(Order).filter_by(user_id=user_id).all()

    # Order Item operations
    def add_order_item(self, order_id, wheel_id, accessory_id, quantity, price):
        order_item = OrderItem(order_id=order_id, wheel_id=wheel_id, accessory_id=accessory_id, quantity=quantity, price=price)
        self.add(order_item)
        return order_item

    # Review operations
    def add_review(self, user_id, wheel_id, rating, comment, review_date):
        review = Review(user_id=user_id, wheel_id=wheel_id, rating=rating, comment=comment, review_date=review_date)
        self.add(review)
        return review

    def get_reviews_by_wheel(self, wheel_id):
        return self.session.query(Review).filter_by(wheel_id=wheel_id).all()

    def get_review_by_id(self, review_id):
        return self.session.query(Review).filter_by(id=review_id).first()

if __name__ == '__main__':

from sqlalchemy import create_engine, Column, Integer, String, Float, ForeignKey, Boolean
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, relationship
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base

Base = declarative_base()

class Wheel(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'wheels'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String)
    description = Column(String)
    price = Column(Float)
    size = Column(Integer)  # Wheel diameter in inches

    brand = Column(String)
    quantity_in_stock = Column(Integer)
    image_url = Column(String) # URL to wheel image

    orders = relationship("OrderItem", back_populates="wheel")

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<Wheel(name='{self.name}', price={self.price})>"

class Accessory(Base):

class User(Base):

class Order(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'orders'

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)

    total_amount = Column(Float)
    shipping_address = Column(String)
    billing_address = Column(String)
    status = Column(String) # e.g., "Pending", "Shipped", "Delivered"

    user = relationship("User", back_populates="orders")
